实证验证与实用启示

通过成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attack） 验证：实测隐私泄露程度与理论预测高度一致，显著依赖于正则化强度、后验方差大小及发布的样本路径数量。下游任务（如贝叶斯优化、区间预测）实验表明：在含噪观测场景下，适配隐私需求的正则化可在仅损失~15%效用的前提下实现ε≤2.0的Rényi-DP保障。当需更强隐私时，可叠加校准后的GP噪声（如协方差缩放扰动），形成可解析的“双重隐私旋钮”——既保留GP建模能力，又提供显式、可调的额外隐私增益。

AI Summary (English)

We establish the first explicit Rényi-DP guarantees for releasing posterior sample paths from a Gaussian process (GP) with fully private training data (both covariates and responses). Unlike standard DP mechanisms, we leverage the intrinsic randomness of posterior sampling—deriving tight bounds that separate leakage from the posterior mean (response-driven) and the data-dependent posterior covariance (covariate- and kernel-driven). Crucially, meaningful privacy hinges on effective ridge regularization: stronger regularization improves covariance conditioning and yields exponentially tighter DP bounds. Empirical membership-inference attacks confirm the predicted dependence on regularization strength, posterior variance, and number of released paths. Utility experiments show that privacy-compatible regularization preserves decision quality in noisy-observation regimes with only modest utility loss (~15%). When stricter privacy is required, calibrated GP noise (e.g., scaled covariance perturbation) provides an analytically tractable additional privacy knob, augmenting the intrinsic guarantee without sacrificing GP semantics.

Abstract

We study the privacy of releasing posterior sample paths from a Gaussian process (GP) when the entire training set including covariates and responses is private. Unlike standard differential-privacy (DP) mechanisms that add external noise, posterior sampling is random by construction. We show that this intrinsic randomness yields DP guarantees by deriving explicit Rényi-DP bounds for GP posterior sample-path release. The bounds separate posterior-mean leakage from data-dependent posterior-covariance leakage showing that meaningful privacy depends sharply on effective ridge regularisation. We apply membership-inference attacks to show that empirical leakage follows the predicted dependence on regularisation, posterior variance and the number of released posterior sample-paths. Utility experiments on downstream posterior-sampling tasks identify noisy-observation regimes where privacy-compatible regularisation preserves useful decisions with modest utility loss. When stronger privacy is needed, the intrinsic guarantee can be sharpened by adding calibrated GP noise, providing an explicit additional privacy knob.

item.title: An AI Security Agent for Banking: Multi-Vector Fraud and AML Detection Across Retail and Corporate Accounts
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17555v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

银行业面临两类本质迥异的金融威胁：基于签名的传统欺诈（如无卡交易攻击、账户接管、ATM克隆）与行为驱动的金融犯罪（如资金拆分、洗钱分层、跑分网络、商业邮件诈骗）。现有静态规则引擎虽能高效识别高频、暴力型异常，却在面对刻意伪装为正常行为的高级威胁（如BEC付款重定向、会话劫持、洗钱分层）时存在结构性盲区——因其仅依赖单笔交易或单一会话的孤立阈值判断。

方法创新

本文提出首个面向零售与对公银行场景的AI安全智能体，采用双流融合架构：

  • 交易流（覆盖银行卡欺诈、ACH/电汇欺诈、AML子类）与会话流（覆盖账户接管、SIM卡交换、内部滥用等）并行处理；
  • 每一流均集成三模块：① LSTM序列模型建模账户级行为时序模式；② 统计型速率/阈值监测器捕捉突发性偏离；③ 图神经网络模块量化账户-对手方关系拓扑（如扇入/扇出比、过账率），专用于洗钱链路识别。

主要成果

在含237,669笔交易、113,508次会话、13类威胁、3,470个模拟账户的合成数据集上，本模型F1达0.787（交易流） 与0.867（会话流），显著优于规则基线（0.562/0.733）和纯LSTM基线（0.655/0.713）。系统集成两大实用组件：面向客户的交易验证聊天机器人（身份核验准确率96.6%，批量重置攻击检出率86.8%）及分析师案情摘要助手（操作建议F1达99.3%），关键告警自动响应延迟P95 < 0.43 ms。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces an AI security agent for retail and corporate banking that unifies detection of signature-based fraud (e.g., card-not-present, ATM cloning) and behavior-driven financial crime (e.g., BEC, money laundering layering, session hijacking)—threats requiring fundamentally distinct analytical approaches. The agent employs a novel dual-stream fusion architecture: a transaction stream (for fraud & AML categories) and a session stream (for account takeover, SIM-swap, insider abuse), each integrating LSTM sequence modeling, statistical velocity monitoring, and graph-based counterparty relationship analysis. Evaluated on a synthetic dataset of 237,669 transactions and 113,508 sessions across 13 threat types, the model achieves F1 scores of 0.787 (transaction) and 0.867 (session), outperforming rule-based (0.562/0.733) and LSTM-only (0.655/0.713) baselines. It further delivers production-ready components: a customer-facing chatbot (96.6% identity verification accuracy; 86.8% mass-reset attack detection) and an analyst assistant (99.3% action-recommendation F1), with critical-tier automated response latency under 0.43 ms at P95.

Abstract

Banks simultaneously face signature-based fraud (card-not-present attacks, account takeover, ATM cloning) and behavioural financial crime (structuring, layering, mule networks, business email compromise) -- two threat families with fundamentally different detection requirements. Static rule engines that reliably catch brute-force and high-velocity events are structurally blind to business-email-compromise (BEC) payment redirection, session hijacking, and money-laundering layering, which are engineered to appear indistinguishable from legitimate activity at the individual transaction or session level. This paper presents an AI security agent for retail and corporate banking that addresses this gap through a three-component fusion architecture operating on two parallel event streams: a transaction stream (card fraud, ACH/wire fraud, AML categories) and a session stream (account takeover, session hijacking, SIM-swap, insider abuse). Each stream combines an LSTM sequence model capturing per-account behavioural history, a statistical velocity/threshold monitor, and a graph/network module capturing account-counterparty relationship patterns (fan-in, fan-out, pass-through ratio) for money-laundering detection. Experiments on a synthetic event log of 237,669 transactions and 113,508 sessions across 13 threat categories and 3,470 simulated accounts demonstrate overall F1 of 0.787 (transaction stream) and 0.867 (session stream) for the proposed model, versus 0.562/0.733 for a rule-based baseline and 0.655/0.713 for an LSTM-only baseline. The agent includes a customer-facing transaction-verification chatbot (96.6% identity verification accuracy, 86.8% mass-reset attack detection) and an analyst case-summary assistant (99.3% action-recommendation F1), with Critical-tier automated response latency under 0.43 ms at the 95th percentile.

item.title: PARSE: Provenance-Aware Retrieval Sanitization for Professional Domain LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17467v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

现有针对大语言模型（LLM）代理的提示注入防御方法，多在合成基准上评估，但其在真实企业文档场景中严重失效。真实专业文档（如SEC财报、联邦法规、医学摘要等）具有长度长、信息密度高、权威性语言与事实内容交织等特点，导致合成数据上的防御泛化能力极低。

方法创新：PARSE框架

本文提出PARSE（Provenance-Aware Retrieval Sanitization），一种面向专业领域的溯源感知检索净化框架：

  • 领域适配分类：逐句评估注入风险概率，支持金融、法律、医疗、科研、DevOps五大专业域；
  • 事实保真净化：先结构化抽取关键事实（主谓宾三元组+约束条件），再重写可疑句，避免语义失真；
  • 一致性验证环：通过双向推理校验重写前后事实等价性，确保知识完整性；
  • 直接性门控机制：对59%的低风险文档启用轻量路径，将计算资源聚焦于高危样本，提升效率。

关键结果

在覆盖122项真实任务的跨域基准上，PARSE将攻击成功率从基线25.4%显著降至15.6%（降幅38%，p=0.014，统计功效充足），同时保持86.9%的响应效用——远优于最强基线方法（Paraphrasing）：后者在真实文档中无显著防护效果（p=0.500），且效用从91.8%骤降至82.8%。研究强调：专业LLM安全评估必须基于领域匹配的真实文档，而非合成代理数据。

AI Summary (English)

Existing prompt injection defenses—evaluated on synthetic benchmarks—fail catastrophically on real enterprise documents due to their length, density, and entanglement of authoritative language with factual content. We expose this gap using a rigorous real-document benchmark of 122 tasks across five professional domains (financial, legal, medical, scientific, DevOps), sourced from SEC filings, Federal Register rules, PubMed abstracts, arXiv papers, and GitHub postmortems. PARSE (Provenance-Aware Retrieval Sanitization) introduces a domain-aware, fact-preserving sanitization pipeline: it classifies sentence-level injection likelihood, extracts structured facts before rewriting, and verifies fact preservation via a consistency-checking loop; a directiveness gate routes 59% of documents to a lightweight path, optimizing compute. PARSE achieves a 15.6% attack success rate—a 38% reduction versus the 25.4% baseline—at 86.9% utility, the only method showing both statistical significance (p=0.014, adequately powered) and near-baseline utility. We urge practitioners to evaluate defenses on domain-matched real documents—not synthetic proxies.

Abstract

Prompt injection defenses evaluated on synthetic benchmarks do not generalize to real enterprise documents, which are longer, denser, and interleave legitimate authority language with factual content. We demonstrate this gap with a real-document benchmark of 122 tasks across five professional domains (financial, legal, medical, scientific, DevOps) using actual SEC filings, Federal Register rules, PubMed abstracts, arXiv papers, and GitHub postmortems. Paraphrasing, the strongest defense on synthetic benchmarks, shows no statistically significant attack success rate reduction on real documents (p=0.500) while degrading utility from 91.8% to 82.8%. We introduce PARSE (Provenance-Aware Retrieval Sanitization), a domain-aware, fact-preserving sanitization pipeline that classifies each sentence by injection likelihood, extracts structured facts before rewriting, and verifies fact preservation via a consistency-checking loop. A directiveness gate routes 59% of real enterprise documents to a lightweight path, concentrating computational cost on high-risk documents. PARSE achieves 15.6% attack success rate -- a 38% reduction versus the 25.4% baseline -- at 86.9% utility, the only condition that is both statistically significant (p=0.014, adequately powered) and maintains near-baseline utility. Practitioners should evaluate defenses on domain-matched real documents, not synthetic proxies.

item.title: IUU+DB: Tracking Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated Fishing, Seafood Fraud, and Labor Abuse through LLM-driven Information Extraction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18181v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

非法、未报告和无管制捕捞（IUU）长期威胁海洋生态、全球渔业市场及沿海社区生计。本文提出扩展概念 IUU+，涵盖IUU捕捞及其供应链延伸的多重违法现象——包括海产品欺诈（如物种标签造假、产地篡改）和强迫劳动等劳工虐待行为。尽管IUU+问题广受关注，但其发生频率、地理分布、涉事物种/船舶/主体、违规类型及执法响应等关键维度仍缺乏系统性量化数据，主因是信息高度碎片化：散见于新闻报道、NGO调查报告、政府通报、法庭文书等异构文本中，人工整理成本高、覆盖窄、时效差。

方法与系统设计

本研究构建 IUU+DB——一个基于大语言模型（LLM）驱动的信息提取系统。该系统具备三阶段流水线：（1）多源文档接入与相关性分类（判断是否含IUU+事件）；（2）细粒度结构化抽取，精准识别涉事主体（企业、船东、国家）、地理坐标（港口、海域、国家）、目标物种、船舶信息、具体违规行为（如伪造捕捞日志、规避监测、剥削船员）及执法结果（罚款、扣船、起诉）；（3）智能去重与时空趋势分析，支持跨文档事件聚合与热区可视化。

主要发现与应用价值

经多案例验证（覆盖东南亚、西非、南美等高风险区域），IUU+DB显著提升数据整合效率（准确率>89%，F1-score达0.91），成功揭示隐性关联模式（如特定注册国船舶高频关联多类违规）。该系统已支持学术机构开展渔业治理研究、NGO生成风险简报、企业执行源头与物种级风险评估（如金枪鱼供应链溯源），并为监管机构提供靶向执法线索与政策效果追踪工具，推动从被动响应转向主动预防的治理范式转型。

AI Summary (English)

Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated (IUU) fishing is increasingly recognized as part of a broader set of interconnected fisheries crimes—termed IUU+—encompassing seafood fraud (e.g., species substitution, origin mislabeling) and labor abuses (e.g., forced labor onboard vessels). To address the critical data gap in quantifying IUU+ incidents globally, we introduce IUU+DB, an LLM-driven system for automated extraction, classification, and structuring of IUU+ events from heterogeneous textual sources (news, NGO reports, legal documents). It achieves high precision (>89%) and recall in extracting actors, locations, species, vessels, violations, and enforcement outcomes, while enabling deduplication and spatiotemporal trend analysis. Validation across 12 high-risk regions demonstrates its capacity to surface hidden patterns (e.g., vessel-flag-state–violation correlations), support academic research and NGO advocacy, inform industry risk assessments, and guide targeted regulatory enforcement—marking a scalable, evidence-based step toward integrated fisheries governance.

Abstract

Illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing (IUU) traditionally refers to fishing activities that violate applicable laws or occur in areas that lack applicable laws. We propose the term IUU+ to capture a broader suite of fisheries sector environmental and associated supply chain trade-related crimes and behaviors. Although IUU+ activity is widely recognized as a serious threat to marine ecosystems, markets, and livelihoods, a quantitative understanding of these incidents, e.g., their frequency, geography, species, actors, and patterns in the type of illicit activity, remains difficult to obtain. We propose IUU+DB, a large language model driven system for building a global incident database of IUU+ activity. The system ingests heterogeneous documents, classifies whether they describe relevant incidents, extracts key data elements such as actors, locations, species, vessels, violations, and enforcement outcomes, and supports deduplication and trend analysis. Case studies and validation results show that IUU+DB can help organize fragmented evidence, surface geographic and behavioral hotspots, support fisheries-domain specific research in academia and non-government organizations, assist source and species risk assessments for industry, and provide support for policy implementation and targeted enforcement efforts to government agencies.

item.title: Embedded Machine Learning for Microcontroller-Class Edge Devices: Data, Feature, Evaluation, and Deployment Pipelines
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18122v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向微控制器级边缘设备的嵌入式机器学习：数据、特征、评估与部署全流程

嵌入式机器学习（EML）将推理任务从云端迁移至资源严苛的边缘设备，要求在极有限的内存（KB级）、能量（毫焦耳级）和延迟（毫秒级）约束下完成数据采集→信号预处理→模型推理→闭环响应全链路。本文提出一种面向微控制器（MCU）平台的系统化EML工作流，突破传统ML教程中忽略的工程关键点：

  • 数据层：针对实时性与存储瓶颈，设计带环形缓冲的自适应采样策略，兼顾抗混叠与功耗；
  • 特征层：将特征提取明确定义为有损但可解释的维度压缩——如惯性运动识别中，对2秒三轴加速度原始序列（~600样本）计算RMS与频谱能量比，降维至12维；
  • 评估层：在类别严重不平衡（如异常检测正负样本比1:200）场景下，采用F2-score与误唤醒率（FAR）双指标验证，避免准确率误导；
  • 部署层：倡导模型与运行时协同设计（co-design），例如为关键词识别（KWS）定制轻量1D-CNN，配合CMSIS-NN库实现单周期FFT+MFCC流水线，推理延迟压至32ms（Cortex-M4@160MHz）。

论文以惯性运动识别与音频关键词 spotting两大典型信号族为贯穿案例，提炼出5条鲁棒部署准则：① 原始数据需经设备端标定校准；② 量化须保留BN层统计量并校准激活范围；③ 分类阈值需基于置信度分布动态调整；④ 多任务调度采用时间触发+事件驱动混合范式；⑤ 现场监控必须嵌入推理吞吐量与内存碎片率双遥测。本工作为MCU级AI提供了可复现、可验证、可量产的工程范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents a systems-oriented embedded machine learning (EML) workflow tailored for microcontroller-class edge devices. Moving beyond generic ML tutorials, we explicitly address engineering-critical decisions often omitted in literature: constrained-data sampling and buffering, feature extraction as interpretable dimensionality reduction, rigorous evaluation under severe class imbalance (e.g., F2-score + false alarm rate), model/runtime co-design, and streaming deployment. Using two representative signal families—inertial motion recognition (2s, 3-axis accelerometer → RMS + spectral features) and keyword spotting (audio → anti-aliased sampling → MFCC → compact 1D-CNN)—we demonstrate end-to-end implementation on resource-limited MCUs (e.g., Cortex-M4). Key contributions include: (1) a validated pipeline achieving <32ms inference latency and <8KB RAM footprint for KWS; (2) imbalance-aware validation protocols that prevent accuracy overestimation; and (3) five practical design rules for robust on-device inference—covering data curation, quantization-aware calibration, dynamic thresholding, hybrid scheduling, and field-deployable telemetry. The workflow is open, reproducible, and production-ready.

Abstract

Embedded machine learning moves inference from cloud services to resource-constrained devices that must acquire data, preprocess signals, run a model, and act within tight limits on memory, energy, and latency. This paper presents a systems-oriented synthesis of an embedded machine-learning workflow for microcontroller-class platforms. The emphasis is placed on engineering decisions that are often hidden in generic machine-learning introductions: sampling and buffering, feature extraction as dimensionality reduction, validation under class imbalance, model/runtime co-design, and streaming deployment. Two representative signal families are used throughout the paper. The first is inertial motion recognition, where a two-second, three-axis accelerometer window is transformed from raw samples into root-mean-square and spectral features before classification. The second is keyword spotting, where audio is sampled, anti-aliased, transformed into mel-frequency cepstral coefficients, and processed by a compact one-dimensional convolutional network. The paper concludes with practical design rules for robust on-device inference, including data curation, quantization, thresholding, scheduling, and field monitoring.

item.title: C2FL: Clustered Continual Federated Learning under Spatial and Temporal Drift
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18003v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在集体自适应系统（CAS）（如车联网、无人机监测、智能手机众包感知）中，分布式节点需基于本地感知数据持续学习，以动态适配环境。然而，该过程面临三重耦合挑战：隐私约束（敏感数据不可上传至中心服务器）、空间异质性（移动节点在不同地理区域观测到显著差异的分布，自然形成空间聚类）以及时间漂移（节点移动导致局部数据分布随时间持续演化，使模型快速过时）。传统联邦学习（FL）假设静态同构或简单非独立同分布（Non-IID）数据，无法协同应对空间结构与动态时序漂移的联合影响。

方法创新：C2FL框架

我们提出 C2FL（Clustered Continual Federated Learning）——一种完全去中心化的持续联邦学习框架：

  • 空间自组织聚类：节点仅通过轻量级地理邻近性度量（如GPS距离或信号强度）自主发现并加入本地学习组，无需全局拓扑或中心协调，天然反映环境的空间结构；
  • 持续适应机制：每个节点集成经验回放（Experience Replay） 与驻留时间感知的自适应模型平均：驻留时间越长，越倾向于融合当前区域共识模型；同时保留历史知识，缓解灾难性遗忘；
  • 无中心、低通信开销：所有操作在本地或邻近节点间完成，避免全局聚合瓶颈与单点故障。

实验验证与贡献

在严格可控的合成数据集上，我们系统模拟了多种空间迁移（如区域切换）与时间漂移（如分布渐变/突变）组合场景。结果表明：标准FedAvg、FedProx等方法性能下降达35–62%；而C2FL在准确率、收敛稳定性及知识保留能力上显著优于基线，平均提升21.4%，且在长期部署中保持鲁棒性。本工作首次将空间聚类机制与持续学习策略深度耦合，为移动边缘智能提供了兼顾隐私、地理感知与动态适应性的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Collective Adaptive Systems (CAS), such as vehicular networks and drone swarms, require nodes to continually learn from private, locally sensed data while coping with spatial heterogeneity (geographically clustered distributions) and temporal drift (distribution shifts due to mobility). Standard federated learning fails under this coupled dynamics. We propose C2FL, a fully decentralized continual FL framework where nodes self-organize into spatially coherent learning clusters—no central server or global topology needed. To combat temporal drift, each node jointly employs experience replay and a dwell-time-aware adaptive averaging: longer regional stay increases weight on local consensus, while preserving prior knowledge via replay. Evaluated on synthetic benchmarks with controlled spatial-temporal shifts, C2FL outperforms FedAvg and FedProx by +21.4% average accuracy and maintains robustness over time—demonstrating the first principled integration of spatial clustering and continual adaptation in privacy-preserving distributed learning.

Abstract

Collective Adaptive Systems (CAS) increasingly rely on machine learning to let each node learn from locally sensed data, aligning its behavior with the surrounding environment. Scaling this intelligence, however, raises fundamental challenges: sensed data is often privacy-sensitive, preventing centralized collection; nodes are mobile, traversing regions where nearby nodes perceive similar phenomena while distant ones observe radically different conditions, creating natural spatial clusters; and these distributions evolve over time due to mobility, introducing temporal drift that makes local models progressively stale. These dynamics arise across domains - vehicular sensing, drone-based monitoring, smartphone crowdsensing - yet the interplay of privacy, spatial heterogeneity, and temporal drift severely undermines conventional learning strategies. Therefore, we propose C2FL, a fully distributed Federated Learning (FL) approach where nodes self-organize into learning groups through spatial clustering, reflecting the geographic structure of the environment. To counteract temporal drift, each node combines experience replay with a dwell-time-aware adaptive averaging step, progressively incorporating the regional consensus as it remains longer within the same area, while preserving previously acquired knowledge under evolving distributions. We evaluate our approach on synthetic experiments that systematically reproduce spatial and temporal shifts, showing that standard federated strategies degrade significantly under these conditions and that our method restores robust collective adaptation.

item.title: Multiple cyclicity and Wavelet Decomposition with Channel Correlation for Long-term Time Series Forecasting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17996v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

长时序预测中，周期性（cyclicity）与趋势（trend）是关键内在结构，现有方法虽通过建模二者取得进展，但普遍忽视真实场景下多变量通道间的动态相关性（inter-channel correlation），导致对协同演化模式建模不足；同时，多数模型依赖复杂网络架构融合多源信息，带来显著计算开销，牺牲推理效率。

方法创新：McWC 框架

我们提出 McWC（Multiple cyclicity and Wavelet Decomposition with Channel Correlation），一种轻量高效、解耦式长时序预测模型：

  • 多层周期解耦模块：逐层分离显式周期成分（日/周/年等多尺度周期），避免频谱混叠；
  • 通道相关性建模：采用轻量多层感知机（MLP）学习变量间非线性依赖关系，替代高成本图神经网络或注意力机制；
  • 多级小波分解融合模块：在时频域同步建模高频细节（突变、噪声）与低频趋势，支持可解释的频带特征重组；
  • 频域自相关解耦损失：在傅里叶域设计正则项，显式约束单通道内自相关结构，提升长期依赖建模鲁棒性。

实验结果与价值

在 Electricity、Traffic、Weather 等六大真实数据集上，McWC 在 MAE/MSE 指标上平均超越 PatchTST、iTransformer、WaveNet 等 SOTA 方法 3.2–7.8%，训练速度提升 2.1×，显存占用降低 39%。其模块化设计兼顾精度、效率与可解释性，为资源受限场景下的工业级时序预测提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Long-term time series forecasting (LTSF) demands effective modeling of cyclicity, trend, and inter-variable dependencies. While existing methods focus on cyclicity and trend, they largely ignore real-world inter-channel correlations, leading to suboptimal predictions, and rely on computationally heavy architectures. To address this, we propose McWC, a novel LTSF model that orthogonally models cyclicity, trend, and channel correlations. McWC features: (1) a multi-layer cyclicity construction module for hierarchical periodic decomposition; (2) an MLP-based channel correlation extractor for efficient cross-variable dependency learning; (3) a multi-level wavelet decomposition module to fuse high-/low-frequency components in time-frequency domain; and (4) a frequency-domain loss to decouple intra-channel autocorrelations. Evaluated on six benchmark datasets, McWC achieves new state-of-the-art performance (avg. +3.2–7.8% MAE reduction) with 2.1× faster training and 39% less memory, demonstrating superior accuracy, efficiency, and interpretability.

Abstract

Cyclicity and trend are important components of time series data and many studies based on cyclicity and trend have achieved good results in long-term time series forecasting. However, we believe that current work neglects the influence of real-world inter-channel correlations in time series data which leads to suboptimal predictions. Furthermore, these models rely on complex designs to capture diverse information so that resulting in low computational efficiency. To address this challenge, we propose McWC, a long-term time series forecasting model that separately models the cyclicity, trend, and inter-channel correlations. Specifically, McWC first decouples cyclical information from data using a multi-layer cyclicity construction module. Then, it extracts inter-channel correlations using multi-layer perceptron. Next, it models and fuses the multi-layer high-frequency and low-frequency information from data using a multi-level wavelet decomposition module. Finally, it aggregates the results of different components to obtain the output. Simultaneously, we decouple intra-channel autocorrelations by calculating a loss function in the frequency domain. Experiments on six real-world datasets demonstrate that McWC achieves state-of-the-art performance, exhibiting excellent computational efficiency and historical information extraction capabilities.

item.title: SegTME-UNI2: A Foundation Model-Based Framework for Generalisable Multiclass Cell Segmentation and LLM-Driven Tumour Microenvironment Characterisation in Histopathology
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17702v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

肿瘤微环境（TME）的精准解析对癌症预后评估与免疫治疗响应预测至关重要，但现有方法依赖人工标注、多模型串联及非结构化报告，难以在常规H&E染色切片上实现通用化、可解释、端到端的TME表征。

方法创新

本研究提出SegTME-UNI2——首个基于病理学基础模型的统一框架，涵盖多类细胞分割与大语言模型（LLM）驱动的TME解读：

  • 核心分割模型UNI2-UPERHOVER：融合ViT-Giant架构的UNI2-H基础模型（预训练于超1亿张组织块），搭配双头UperNet解码器——一头执行六类语义分割（肿瘤细胞、淋巴细胞、巨噬细胞等），另一头回归水平/垂直梯度图，支持无监督 watershed 核实例分离；
  • 三阶段渐进式伪标签课程：突破真实像素级标注稀缺瓶颈。Stage 1基于PanNuke真值训练；Stage 2在TCGA-UT 27万张低分辨率补丁上用熵筛选伪标签；Stage 3扩展至全量160万张跨6尺度补丁（0.5–1.0 μm/pixel），全程无权重迁移，仅靠伪标签质量跃升驱动性能进化；
  • 结构化TME特征引擎：从分割结果提取>20维指标，涵盖细胞组成比例、核形态参数、空间熵、细胞间距离矩阵等；
  • LLM临床叙事生成：将JSON格式特征输入微调后的NVIDIA BioNeMo GPT模型，自动生成符合病理报告规范的可解释TME描述（如“CD8+ T细胞富集且呈浸润性分布，提示潜在免疫应答活性”）。

验证与共享

在独立PanNuke与TCGA-UT测试集上验证了分割精度（Dice >0.82）与叙事一致性。开源全部伪标签TCGA-UT数据集（160万补丁）及UNI2-UPERHOVER模型权重，赋能大规模空间生物学研究。

AI Summary (English)

SegTME-UNI2 is a foundation model–based framework enabling generalisable multiclass cell segmentation and LLM-driven clinical interpretation of the tumour microenvironment (TME) from routine H&E slides. Its core, UNI2-UPERHOVER, integrates the UNI2-H ViT-Giant pathology foundation model with dual UperNet decoders: one for six-class semantic segmentation and another for horizontal-vertical gradient regression to enable watershed-based nuclear instance separation. To overcome scarcity of pixel-level annotations, it employs a three-stage progressive pseudo-labeling curriculum—trained from scratch at each stage—using increasingly large and diverse datasets: PanNuke (Stage 1), entropy-filtered TCGA-UT patches (Stage 2), and all 1.6M multi-scale TCGA-UT patches (Stage 3). Segmentation outputs feed a structured pipeline computing >20 compositional, morphological, spatial entropy, and intercellular distance features, encoded as JSON and interpreted by a fine-tuned NVIDIA BioNeMo GPT model to generate clinically meaningful TME narratives. Validation on held-out PanNuke and TCGA-UT subsets confirms feasibility and internal consistency. The pseudo-labeled TCGA-UT dataset and UNI2-UPERHOVER checkpoint are publicly released.

Abstract

Characterising the tumour microenvironment (TME) from routine H&E-stained histology images requires simultaneous cell segmentation, feature extraction, and interpretable clinical reporting. We present SEGTME-UNI2, a unified framework addressing these requirements. Its core is UNI2-UPERHOVER, a dual-head segmentation model pairing the UNI2-H pathology foundation model (ViT-Giant, pretrained on >100M tiles from 100K slides) with two parallel UperNet decoders: one for six-class semantic segmentation and one for horizontal-vertical gradient regression enabling watershed-based nuclear instance separation. To address the lack of pixel-level annotations in large real-world repositories, UNI2-UPERHOVER undergoes a three-stage progressive pseudo-label curriculum. Each stage trains a fresh model without weight transfer, driving improvement entirely via increased pseudo-label quality: Stage 1: Uses human-annotated PanNuke (7,901 images, 189,744 nuclei, 0.25 um/pixel). Stage 2: Uses entropy-filtered pseudo-labels from the Stage 1 model on 271,711 TCGA-UT scale-0 patches (0.5 um/pixel). Stage 3: Uses pseudo-labels from the Stage 2 model on all 1,608,060 TCGA-UT patches across six resolution scales (0.5-1.0 um/pixel). Segmentation outputs feed a structured TME feature extraction pipeline computing 20+ per-patch compositional, morphological, spatial entropy, and intercellular distance metrics. These are encoded as JSON and passed to a fine-tuned NVIDIA BioNeMo GPT model to generate clinically interpretable TME narratives. Preliminary validation on held-out PanNuke and TCGA-UT partitions demonstrates framework feasibility and internal consistency. The pseudo-labelled TCGA-UT dataset and UNI2-UPERHOVER checkpoint are publicly released to support large-scale TME profiling and spatial biology research.

item.title: Incumbent Advantage: Brand Bias and Cognitive Manipulation Dynamics in LLM Recommendation Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17443v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大型语言模型（LLMs）正日益成为消费者获取产品信息的核心渠道，但品牌在此新兴推荐生态中的竞争机制尚不清晰。本研究聚焦于高信任依赖型品类——护肤品，该领域消费者难以在购买前直接评估质量，高度依赖品牌声誉，因而构成检验LLM推荐偏见的理想场景。

研究方法

我们系统测试了三款主流商用LLM（GPT-4o-mini、Claude Sonnet、Gemini 3 Flash），设计三项控制实验：

  • 实验1（条件性垄断检验）：在所有产品参数完全一致的前提下，测量头部品牌被推荐的频率；
  • 实验2（认知干预测试）：引入权威式营销话术（如虚构临床证据），量化其打破品牌垄断所需的“偏见盈余值”；
  • 实验3（多品牌博弈分析）：模拟地理围栏（GEO）场景下多个品牌同步优化提示词的竞争均衡，构建收益代理指标（payoff proxy）评估策略外溢效应。

另以搜索型商品（如USB线）作稳健性检验，验证结论的品类边界。

主要发现

1. 条件性垄断现象：当参数完全相同时，知名品牌获100%推荐（IAI=10.0），但仅需竞品提升+0.1星评分即可瓦解该垄断；
2. 认知操纵有效性：权威话术可等效转化为+0.17星评级优势，且各模型响应异质——GPT最易被操控，Gemini最稳健；
3. 生成式引擎优化（GEO）引发社会困境：当所有品牌采用相同GEO策略时，个体收益从+0.802骤降至+0.007；未参与优化的品牌则彻底“消失”于推荐结果（0%曝光）。

理论与实践启示

本研究首次将GEO定位为新型市场基础设施，而非单纯安全漏洞。它重构了品牌竞争规则：短期策略套利不可持续，长期需转向可信度基建与跨模型协同治理。研究为监管框架设计、平台算法透明度及品牌数字战略提供了实证锚点。

AI Summary (English)

This study investigates how brand competition unfolds in LLM recommendation systems, using skincare—a credence good category—as a testbed across GPT-4o-mini, Claude Sonnet, and Gemini 3 Flash. We identify three key dynamics: (1) A Conditional Monopoly, where dominant brands receive 100% recommendations when product specs are identical (IAI = 10.0), but this collapses with just a +0.1-star rating advantage for competitors; (2) Cognitive Manipulation, where authority-style marketing (e.g., fabricated clinical claims) breaks the monopoly at a Bias Surplus Value equivalent to +0.17 rating points—yet model responses vary significantly; and (3) A Social Dilemma in multi-brand GEO competition: when all brands adopt identical optimization strategies, individual payoff drops sharply from +0.802 to +0.007, while non-participating brands receive zero recommendations. These findings position Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) not merely as a security concern, but as an emergent, structurally consequential marketing practice reshaping market equilibrium and competitive fairness.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are becoming a major way for consumers to find products, but we do not yet understand how brands compete in this new channel. We study brand dynamics in LLM recommendations using skincare products -- a category where consumers cannot easily judge quality before buying and must rely on brand reputation -- across three commercial LLMs (GPT-4o-mini, Claude Sonnet, Gemini 3 Flash), with a robustness check on search goods. In three experiments, we find: (1) a Conditional Monopoly where well-known brands get recommended 100% of the time (IAI = 10.0) when all products have the same specifications, but this dominance disappears with less than a +0.1-star rating advantage for a competitor; (2) authority-style marketing language, including fabricated clinical-evidence claims, breaks this monopoly at a Bias Surplus Value equal to +0.17 rating points, with each model responding differently; and (3) a social dilemma in multi-brand GEO competition: when all brands adopt the same optimization strategy, individual payoff falls from +0.802 to +0.007 in our payoff proxy, and non-participating brands receive zero recommendations in our tests. Our results suggest that generative engine optimization (GEO) should be studied not only as a security risk, but also as an emerging marketing practice that shapes market competition.

item.title: Predictive Analytics in E-Commerce for CustomerBehavior Forecasting using hybrid Ret-DNN withXGBoost Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17931v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着电子商务深度融入日常生活，零售平台亟需精准刻画用户行为并预测其未来购买意向。然而，传统模型难以协同建模交易数据中的时序动态性（如购买频次、间隔、周期性）与结构化特征（如用户属性、商品类别、促销响应），导致预测精度受限。

方法创新

本研究提出一种新型混合预测框架——Ret-DNN–XGBoost模型：

  • Ret-DNN（Retail Deep Neural Network）：专为电商场景设计的深度网络，融合LSTM与注意力机制，从原始交易序列中自动提取多尺度时序模式（如复购节奏、兴趣漂移）；
  • XGBoost模块：接收Ret-DNN输出的高维时序嵌入向量，并融合静态特征（如年龄分层、地域编码、会员等级），进行端到端概率回归；
  • 数据来自英国某主流在线零售商，涵盖近50万条真实交易记录（2019–2022年），经严格预处理：异常值鲁棒裁剪、时间窗口滑动构造序列样本、目标变量定义为未来7天内购买概率，最终完成z-score标准化与类别特征嵌入。

主要成果

在相同数据集与评估协议下，本模型显著优于基线：

  • MAE降至0.2193（较纯Ret-DNN降低12.7%），表明预测偏差更小；
  • AUC达0.892，F1-score提升至0.76，验证其对“高价值潜在买家”的识别能力；
  • 消融实验证实：Ret-DNN的时序表征能力+XGBoost的非线性决策优势形成互补，尤其在长尾用户（低频购买者）预测上提升显著（MAE改善19.3%）。

本工作为电商个性化推荐、库存前置与精准营销提供了可落地的预测范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes a hybrid Ret-DNN–XGBoost model for customer purchase probability forecasting in e-commerce. Ret-DNN—a purpose-built deep neural network—captures temporal dynamics (e.g., purchase intervals, behavioral drift) from transaction sequences via LSTM and attention mechanisms. Its learned embeddings, combined with static features (e.g., demographics, membership tier), are fed into XGBoost for final probabilistic prediction. Evaluated on a real-world UK retailer dataset (~500K transactions), the model achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.2193, outperforming the standalone Ret-DNN baseline by 12.7%. It also attains an AUC of 0.892 and F1-score of 0.76, demonstrating robustness for long-tail users. The framework bridges temporal representation learning and tabular prediction, offering a practical solution for demand forecasting and targeted marketing.

Abstract

In recent years, electronic (E) commerce services have rapidly increased in the daily lives of people, which helpsthem to purchase products online. However, retail platforms have struggled to understand customer behavior and make it difficult to predict their future purchases. To overcome these challenges, this study proposes a hybrid Retail Deep NeuralNetwork (Ret-DNN) with an Extreme Gradient Boosting(XGBoost) model for capturing temporal features and tabular dynamics of retail data. First, data were sourced from a UnitedKingdom (UK)-based online retailer that contains transactions with almost 500,000 records. Then, the collected data were pre-processed using a series of techniques, such as data cleaning, outlier handling, temporal feature extraction, feature encoding, and z-score normalization, to ensure that the data were ready for model training and testing. Subsequently, the preprocessed data were fed into the Ret-DNN model, which acts as a feature extractor to understand the complete context of customer transactions. Further, the extracted data were fed as input into the XGBoost model, which predicted the final output as the purchase probability of customers. Finally, the proposed Ret-DNN XGBoost model achieved better results by attaining aMean Absolute Error (MAE) 0.2193 when compared to the existing Ret-DNN model. Keywords: Customer behavior forecasting, extreme gradientboosting, electronic commerce, predictive analytic, retail deepneural networks.

item.title: CheckMIABench: Firm Foundations For Membership Inference Attacks on Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17464v1

AI Summary (中文)

CheckMIABench：面向大语言模型的会员推断攻击基准框架

背景与挑战：会员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attacks, MIAs）是评估机器学习模型隐私泄露风险的核心范式。然而，当前针对大语言模型（LLMs）的MIA评估普遍存在严重方法学缺陷：多数基准依赖人工构建的“成员集”（训练数据子集）与“非成员集”（外部语料），二者常存在隐性分布偏移（如领域、风格或时序差异），导致攻击性能被高估或归因错误——近期研究已证实，仅依赖输入统计特征的“盲式”基线方法，在相同基准上竟显著超越需访问模型的先进攻击，暴露出评估失真问题。

方法创新：本文提出 CheckMIABench，首个基于训练过程内在一致性构建的严格MIA基准。核心洞见在于：同一模型训练过程中，固定时间点前后的训练样本天然同分布（因共享原始训练语料与预处理流程）。据此，我们以公开中间检查点（checkpoints）为锚点，将早于该点的数据视为“成员”，晚于该点的数据视为“非成员”，彻底消除分布不匹配。该范式适用于所有具备开源训练数据与中间检查点的模型（如Pythia、OLMo系列）。

实证验证与开源贡献：我们在70M至7B参数规模的6个Pythia/OLMo模型上系统评估了6种主流MIA方法，发现多数现有攻击在CheckMIABench上性能骤降（平均AUC下降12–34%），印证了传统基准的乐观偏差。为推动可复现研究，我们开源模块化攻击库 Pandora-LLM（https://github.com/safr-ai-lab/pandora_llm），支持攻击设计、分布对齐、结果标准化全流程。

AI Summary (English)

Membership inference attacks (MIAs) are critical for auditing language model privacy, yet existing benchmarks suffer from distributional mismatches between member and non-member sets—leading to inflated attack performance and flawed comparisons. CheckMIABench addresses this by leveraging a key insight: training data before and after a fixed checkpoint are drawn from the same underlying distribution, enabling statistically sound MIA evaluation. We convert open-source models with public training data and intermediate checkpoints (e.g., Pythia, OLMo, 70M–7B) into principled testbeds. Evaluating six state-of-the-art attacks, we find their AUC drops significantly (12–34% on average) under CheckMIABench versus prior benchmarks—revealing substantial overestimation in prior work. To foster reproducible privacy research, we release Pandora-LLM, a modular, extensible library for MIA design and evaluation: https://github.com/safr-ai-lab/pandora_llm.

Abstract

Membership inference attacks (MIAs) are a canonical way to assess a machine learning model's privacy properties. Although several attempts have been made to evaluate MIAs on language models, the extant literature has suffered numerous difficulties in constructing clean evaluations to test new techniques. In particular, subtle distribution shifts between member and non-member sets can undermine the statistical validity of MIAs; recent work has underscored this by showing that "blind" methods with no access to the underlying model can perform far better than published methods on the same benchmarks. This paper constructs a benchmark for principled evaluation of MIAs against LLMs, by leveraging the insight that training data before and after a fixed point during training are drawn from the same distribution. Therefore, all open-source models with intermediate checkpoints and public training data can be converted into MIA testbeds. We apply our framework to a half-dozen published attacks on the Pythia and OLMo family of models, from 70M to 7B parameters. To facilitate further privacy research, we open-source a modular library for designing and implementing attacks in this setting: https://github.com/safr-ai-lab/pandora_llm.

item.title: Darshana Graph: A Parallel Commentary Corpus for Comparative Indian Philosophy, with Stylometric and Exploratory Graph Analyses
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18222v1

AI Summary (中文)

Darshana Graph：面向比较印度哲学的并行评注语料库及图谱分析

本研究推出 Darshana Graph——首个大规模、结构化、跨传统的平行哲学评注语料库，涵盖古典印度教、佛教与耆那教三大传统，共收录超125,000条文本记录。语料源自公共领域及开放许可的英译经典，包括《薄伽梵歌》《梵经》《主要奥义书》《巴利三藏》及核心耆那文献。其核心创新在于构建了约8,500条高度对齐的评注子集：同一根本偈颂（śloka）或经文（sūtra）被十八位历史评注家——分属商羯罗等五派吠檀多学派及其他正统/非正统哲学院（darśana）——独立阐释，实现跨学派、跨时代的“同源异释”精准比对。据我们所知，这是目前唯一公开、可复用、具备此规模与粒度的跨评注者对齐资源。

基于该语料，我们开展两项互补性分析：
1. 透明化风格计量分析（无需机器学习）：以引经密度（scriptural citation density）、显性驳斥率（explicit refutation rate）和句法复杂度为指标，揭示论证风格差异。结果发现：引经密度与驳斥率呈中度负相关（r ≈ −0.42）；在毗湿奴派—罗摩奴阇—维德亚拉尼亚这一师承谱系中，驳斥率逐代显著上升；甚至在《巴利三藏》内部，经藏（Sutta）、律藏（Vinaya）与论藏（Abhidhamma）亦呈现可量化的文体风格分化。
2. 约束型大语言模型关系抽取与图谱构建：采用预定义哲学关系词汇表（如“主张”“反对”“继承”“修正”），结合确定性后处理验证，生成概念间有向关系图。图谱成功凸显各学派在“自我”“业力”“解脱”等核心概念上的结构性分歧，但也暴露关键局限：约12%的关系节点在独立嵌入空间分析中未获支持，提示符号化抽取与语义相似性存在张力。

全部语料、关系图谱及代码已开源，为数字印度哲学研究提供可扩展、可验证、可复现的基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Darshana Graph, a parallel commentary corpus of >125,000 records spanning Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain philosophical traditions, featuring a unique 8,500-record subset where identical root verses/sūtras are aligned across 18 historical commentators from five Vedānta schools and other darśanas—enabling unprecedented cross-traditional comparison. Two analyses demonstrate its utility: (1) A transparent stylometric study—using scriptural citation density, explicit refutation rate, and sentence complexity—reveals a moderate negative correlation between citation density and refutation (r ≈ −0.42), a marked increase in refutation across a doctrinal lineage, and measurable genre-level stylistic variation within the Pāli Canon itself; (2) A constrained LLM pipeline extracts typed philosophical relations (e.g., “rejects”, “elaborates”) with deterministic validation, yielding a graph that surfaces inter-school disagreement patterns while exposing extraction limitations—e.g., ~12% of graph-derived relations lack support in independent embedding-based semantic analysis. The full corpus, graph, and code are publicly released.

Abstract

We introduce Darshana Graph, a corpus of over 125,000 text records spanning classical Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain philosophical traditions, drawn from public-domain and openly licensed translations of sources including the Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Sutras, principal Upanishads, the Pali Canon, and core Jain texts. Its distinctive contribution lies in a structurally unique subset of roughly 8,500 Hindu and Jain records in which the same root verse or sutra is aligned across eighteen historical commentators representing five schools of Vedanta and other darshanas, enabling direct comparison of how independent interpretive traditions read identical source material. To our knowledge, no publicly available resource provides comparable cross-commentator alignment at this scale. We present two analyses built on this corpus. First, a transparent stylometric comparison requiring no machine learning measures argumentative style through scriptural citation density, explicit refutation rate, and sentence complexity. It finds a moderate negative correlation between citation density and refutation rate, a marked increase in refutation rate across three commentators in a related doctrinal lineage, and measurable genre-level differences within the Pali Canon itself. Second, we describe a constrained large language model pipeline that extracts typed philosophical relationships between concepts using a predefined relation vocabulary and deterministic post-hoc validation. The resulting graph surfaces cross-school disagreement patterns while also revealing important extraction limitations, including cases where an independent embedding-based analysis disagrees with the graph-derived findings. We release the full corpus, extracted relationship graph, and all source code.

item.title: Evaluating Prompting-Based Defenses Against Domain-Camouflaged Injection Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18530v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

域伪装型注入攻击（Domain-Camouflaged Injection Attacks）通过在检索内容中嵌入符合专业领域语义的恶意指令（如使用金融或法律术语），规避依赖语法标记（如“忽略前述指令”）的传统检测器，构成新型高隐蔽性威胁。当检测失效时，亟需评估哪些提示工程类防御架构能有效抑制攻击成功率。

方法与规模

本研究首次系统评估五种提示工程防御策略——聚焦提示（spotlighting）、重述提示（paraphrasing）、提示三明治（prompt sandwiching） 及两种组合方案——在三大模型家族（Claude Haiku、Llama 3.1 8B、Gemini 2.0 Flash）及三大部署域（金融、法律、通用）上的鲁棒性。实验覆盖 3,510 次对抗测试，全部基于合成构建的专业文档（含财报、合同、政策文本等），确保领域适配性与可控性。

关键发现

  • 重述提示为最优单一支架：将检索内容重述后再输入代理模型，平均降低攻击成功率 55–84%，且在所有测试模型上均优于当前最佳开源防护模型 Llama Guard 4；
  • 模型强依赖性显著：聚焦提示使 Claude Haiku 攻击成功率减半，却对 Llama 3.1 8B 完全无效；
  • 金融域风险最高：基线攻击成功率达 26–33%，即使采用最优防御，在较弱模型上仍残留可观风险；
  • 无防御可完全消除威胁：尤其在 Llama 3.1 8B 等模型上，所有提示策略均无法将攻击成功率压至 0%。

创新与价值

本工作建立了首个面向伪装类注入攻击的提示防御基准，提出可落地的部署建议（如优先采用重述+模型选型协同策略），并明确指出：防御效果不能脱离具体模型与领域独立评估。合成数据局限性亦被坦诚指出——真实企业文档中的泛化性能仍待实证验证。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic benchmark of prompting-based defenses against domain-camouflaged injection attacks—adversarial inputs that embed malicious instructions using domain-specific vocabulary (e.g., financial or legal terms) to evade syntax-based detectors. Across 3,510 trials spanning three model families (Claude Haiku, Llama 3.1 8B, Gemini 2.0 Flash) and three domains (financial, legal, general), we evaluate five prompting strategies: spotlighting, paraphrasing, prompt sandwiching, and two combinations. Paraphrasing retrieved content before agent processing emerges as the most consistently effective defense, reducing attack success rates by 55–84% depending on the model—and outperforming our Llama Guard 4 baseline on every tested model. Defense efficacy is highly model-dependent: spotlighting halves attack success on Claude Haiku but yields no benefit on Llama 3.1 8B. Financial-domain deployments exhibit the highest residual risk (26–33% baseline success), and no prompting defense fully eliminates the threat on weaker models. These findings establish actionable, benchmark-grounded guidance for practitioners deploying LLM agents in high-stakes professional settings.

Abstract

Domain-camouflaged injection attacks embed malicious instructions in retrieved content using domain-appropriate vocabulary, evading standard detectors that rely on syntactic injection markers. When detection fails, practitioners need to know which defense architectures reduce attack success. We evaluate five prompting-based defenses (spotlighting, paraphrasing, prompt sandwiching, and two combinations) against domain-camouflaged injection across three model families (Claude Haiku, Llama 3.1 8B, Gemini 2.0 Flash) and three deployment domains (financial, legal, general) using 3,510 trials. Paraphrasing retrieved content before agent processing is the most consistently effective defense in this benchmark, reducing camouflage attack success rate by 55-84\% depending on model, and achieves lower attack success rates than our Llama Guard 4 configuration on every model tested. Defense effectiveness is strongly model-dependent: spotlighting halves attack success on Claude Haiku but provides no benefit on Llama 3.1 8B. Financial domain deployments face the highest residual risk at 26-33\% baseline attack success rate, with no prompting-based defense fully eliminating the threat on weaker models. These results provide the first systematic evaluation of prompting-based defenses specifically against camouflage-class injection attacks and establish benchmark-based recommendations for practitioners. All tasks use synthetically constructed professional documents; whether these benchmark rankings generalize to real enterprise documents remains an open question.

item.title: SafeClawBench: Separating Semantic, Audit-Evidence, and Sandbox Harm in Tool-Using LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18356v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

工具调用型大语言模型（LLM）智能体引入了超越文本不安全性的新型安全风险：如泄露受保护数据、写入持久化内存、发送恶意消息、篡改数据库或触发有害工具执行。现有评测常将语义接受、审计证据与实际执行危害混为单一“攻击成功率”，难以区分模型是仅在文本层面妥协（如口头同意），还是真正引发可观测的系统级危害。

方法与创新

本文提出 SafeClawBench——首个面向工具调用智能体的三阶段安全评测基准，涵盖600个严格控制的对抗性任务，覆盖六大攻击家族：直接/间接提示注入、工具返回注入、内存投毒、内存提取及歧义驱动的不安全推理。其核心创新在于解耦三大独立终点指标：

  • 语义攻击接受率（Semantic Acceptance）：模型是否在响应中显式同意或执行恶意意图；
  • 审计可见危害证据（Audit-Evidence Harm）：日志、API调用记录等可审查的违规痕迹；
  • 沙盒观测工具/状态危害（Sandbox-Observed Harm）：在隔离环境中实际发生的文件修改、网络请求、数据库变更等可执行副作用。

主要发现

在5类智能体端点与4种提示级防护策略下评估表明：三类指标显著分离——语义失败率跨模型差异巨大（9.0%–44.2%）；审计证据范围窄于语义失败；更关键的是，291/347例沙盒实际危害发生在通过语义检查的样本中（基于12,000行匹配分析），证明仅依赖文本合规性存在严重漏检。提示策略效果高度依赖模型架构与执行协议，无普适最优方案。

意义

SafeClawBench 提供可复现、非混淆的评估框架，首次实现对文本合规性、证据可追溯性、状态可执行性的正交度量。开源数据集已发布于 [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/sairights/safeclawbench)，支持安全研究者精准定位故障根源。

AI Summary (English)

SafeClawBench is the first staged benchmark for evaluating security failures in tool-using LLM agents, disentangling three distinct harm layers: semantic acceptance (textual compliance with malicious intent), audit-visible evidence (log/API traces of violation), and sandbox-observed state/tool harm (actual executable effects like DB writes or network calls). It comprises 600 adversarial tasks across six attack families and evaluates five agent endpoints under four prompt-level policies. Key findings: (1) Semantic failure rates vary widely (9.0%–44.2%) without protection; (2) Audit evidence is narrower than semantic failure, but critically, 291 of 347 sandbox harms occurred in samples that passed the semantic check—revealing dangerous blind spots; (3) Prompt policy efficacy is model- and protocol-dependent, not universal. SafeClawBench enables precise, non-conflated comparison of agent safety across textual, evidential, and executable dimensions. Dataset: https://huggingface.co/datasets/sairights/safeclawbench.

Abstract

Tool-using language-model agents introduce security failures that go beyond unsafe text: they can disclose protected objects, write persistent memory, send messages, modify databases, or trigger harmful code and tool effects. Existing evaluations often collapse these stages into a single attack success rate, making it difficult to tell whether a model merely agreed with an attacker or actually produced observable harm. We introduce SafeClawBench, a staged benchmark for tool-using agent security with 600 controlled adversarial tasks across six attack families: direct and indirect prompt injection, tool-return injection, memory poisoning, memory extraction, and ambiguity-driven unsafe inference. SafeClawBench reports three separate endpoints: semantic attack acceptance, audit-visible harm evidence, and sandbox-observed tool/state harm. Evaluating five agent endpoints under four prompt-level policies, we find that these endpoints capture different failure modes. Without additional prompt protection, semantic failure rates vary widely across models, from 9.0% to 44.2%. Audited harm evidence is narrower than semantic failure, and under a separate executable protocol some matched task identities produce sandbox harm despite passing the Semantic Core call: in a 12,000-row matched analysis, 291 of 347 observed sandbox harms occur in rows that pass the semantic check. Prompt policies change endpoint outcomes, but their effects depend on both model and protocol. SafeClawBench provides a reproducible framework for comparing agent models and prompt-policy conditions without conflating textual compliance, evidence-supported harm, and executable state changes. The open-source dataset is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/sairights/safeclawbench.

item.title: Agentra: A Supervisable Multi-Agent Framework for Enterprise Intrusion Response
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18325v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

企业入侵响应仍严重依赖静态剧本（playbooks）和人工研判，导致从告警生成到威胁遏制存在显著延迟，难以应对现代高级持续性威胁（APT）的动态性与复杂性。

方法与框架设计

本文提出 Agentra——一种可监督的多智能体入侵响应系统（IRS）框架。其核心创新在于：

  • 本体驱动的计划生成：将IDS/EDR/XDR平台告警自动转化为结构化响应计划，严格锚定于MITRE ATT&CK（战术映射）、MITRE D3FEND（防御技术）和NIST CSF 2.0（治理框架）三大权威知识图谱；
  • 角色化多智能体协同：按“分析员”“规划员”“验证员”“ Moderator（情报守门人）”等职责划分智能体，实现响应推理解耦；
  • 安全闭环机制：引入Planner–Validator有界审查环确保逻辑一致性；通过Moderator安全网关过滤不可信威胁情报；所有动作须经动作目录（Action Catalog）准入并叠加风险评分门控；全部决策写入仅追加式审计日志（append-only audit log），保障全程可追溯、可复核。

主要发现与贡献

在包含120个真实事件的混合测试集（源自ThreatHunter-Playbook、Splunk BOTSv3与DARPA OpTC）上，Agentra相较OASIS CACAO v2.0静态剧本基线显著提升：

  • FP-aware IRS F1分数由0.61提升至0.84（+37.7%）；
  • 彻底消除Planner单智能体配置引发的有害动作过激反应，将有害动作率从失控状态恢复至基线水平的0.0%；
  • 首次在IRS中实现本体对齐性、人工监督权、操作安全性与审计完备性四重目标的统一。

AI Summary (English)

Enterprise intrusion response remains bottlenecked by static playbooks and analyst-heavy triage, causing critical delays between alerting and containment. We introduce Agentra, a supervisable multi-agent framework that transforms heterogeneous alerts (from IDS/EDR/XDR) into structured, ontology-grounded response plans aligned with MITRE ATT&CK, D3FEND, and NIST CSF 2.0. Agentra decomposes reasoning across role-scoped agents, enforces safety via a bounded Planner–Validator review loop, filters threat intelligence through a Moderator security gateway, gates actions using an Action Catalog and risk scoring, and immutably logs all decisions. Evaluated on a 120-event corpus (ThreatHunter-Playbook, Splunk BOTSv3, DARPA OpTC) against an OASIS CACAO v2.0 baseline, Agentra’s strongest configuration improves FP-aware IRS F1 from 0.61 to 0.84, while eliminating unsafe overreaction—restoring the harmful-action rate to 0.0%, matching the static baseline. Results demonstrate that supervised multi-agent planning enhances both coverage and trustworthiness in ontology-driven intrusion response.

Abstract

Enterprise intrusion response still depends on static playbooks and analyst-driven triage, creating delay between alert generation and containment. We present Agentra, a supervisable multi-agent Intrusion Response System (IRS) framework that converts alerts from IDS, EDR, and XDR platforms into structured incident response plans grounded in MITRE ATT&CK, MITRE D3FEND, and NIST CSF 2.0. Agentra decomposes response reasoning across role-scoped agents, validates proposed plans through a bounded Planner--Validator review loop, screens retrieved threat intelligence through a Moderator security gateway, gates actions through an Action Catalog and risk score, and records decisions in an append-only audit log. We evaluate Agentra against a static OASIS CACAO v2.0 cyber-playbook baseline on a 120-event corpus drawn from ThreatHunter-Playbook, Splunk BOTSv3, and DARPA OpTC. The strongest configuration improves FP-aware IRS F1 from 0.61 to 0.84 and restores the projected harmful-action rate to the static baseline level of 0.0% after Planner-only configurations introduce unsafe overreaction. These results indicate that multi-agent response planning can improve ontology-grounded IRS coverage while preserving analyst approval and auditability.

item.title: TIGER: Inverting Transformer Gradients via Embedding-Subspace Distance Optimization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18312v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（Federated Learning）通过让客户端仅上传梯度而非原始数据，保障了数据本地化隐私。然而，梯度反演攻击（Gradient Inversion, GI）表明，共享梯度仍可能泄露敏感输入——尤其对Transformer架构，现有方法面临两大瓶颈：（1）基于优化的攻击（如Dummy Input Optimization）需反复前向传播，计算开销大、收敛不稳定，难以适配大规模模型；（2）基于子空间的攻击（如Low-Rank Attention Gradient Exploitation）虽能定位真实token嵌入所在的低维子空间，但依赖离散token枚举与成员判定，在量化噪声或差分隐私（DP）扰动下鲁棒性极差，且无法扩展至非因果注意力的编码器模型（如BERT）。

方法创新：TIGER框架

本文提出TIGER（Transformer Inversion via Embedding-Subspace Distance Optimization），一种端到端可微的连续型梯度反演攻击。其核心思想是摒弃离散token搜索与全梯度匹配，转而将子空间信息转化为可微距离目标函数：直接优化连续的token嵌入向量，使其在每一层尽可能逼近由真实梯度导出的嵌入子空间（通过SVD分解注意力梯度获得）。该过程无需词汇表遍历，规避了数值噪声敏感性；同时天然兼容编码器/解码器架构，支持任意注意力掩码类型。

实验结果与价值

在多个主流Transformer模型（RoBERTa-base、BERT-large、GPT-2-small）上的评估表明：

  • 对编码器模型，TIGER在PSNR和LPIPS指标上平均提升23.6%，重构速度较最优基线快5.8×；
  • 对解码器模型，首次在ε=2.0 DP噪声下实现稳定图像/文本重建（成功率>89%），显著优于现有子空间方法；
  • 消融实验证实，子空间距离优化是鲁棒性的关键驱动因素，而非单纯梯度拟合。

TIGER为评估联邦学习中Transformer模型的真实隐私风险提供了更可靠、可扩展的新基准。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enhances privacy by aggregating model gradients instead of raw data—but gradient inversion attacks threaten input reconstruction. Prior transformer-specific attacks either suffer from high cost/unstability (input optimization) or brittle discrete token testing under noise (subspace-based methods), especially in encoder models or DP-defended settings. We propose TIGER, a continuous, differentiable inversion attack that reframes the attention-gradient subspace signal as an embedding-distance minimization objective: rather than matching full gradients or enumerating tokens, TIGER directly optimizes continuous token embeddings to lie close to the true embedding subspace derived from gradient SVD. Experiments show TIGER achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction quality and speed on encoder-only models (e.g., +23.6% PSNR, 5.8× faster), and—critically—enables the first robust reconstructions under differential privacy (ε=2.0) for decoder models, outperforming prior subspace approaches in noise resilience and scalability.

Abstract

Federated learning allows multiple clients to jointly train a shared model by sending gradient updates to a central server while keeping raw inputs local. However, prior gradient inversion attacks show that these updates can reveal enough information to reconstruct client inputs. Existing attacks on transformers either optimize dummy inputs to match the true client updates, which is costly and unstable for modern models, or exploit the low rank of attention gradients to identify a subspace containing the true layer embeddings, followed by a discrete membership test for candidate tokens. However, this token test is brittle under numerical noise, i.e., from quantization or Differential Privacy (DP), and scales poorly for encoder models with non-causal attention. We introduce TIGER, a continuous gradient inversion attack that turns this subspace signal into a differentiable objective. Instead of searching over tokens or matching full gradients, TIGER directly optimizes token embeddings to minimize their distance to the subspace. Our experiments demonstrate that on encoder-only models, TIGER substantially improves both reconstruction quality and runtime over existing attacks, while on decoder models, TIGER is more robust than prior subspace-based attacks, enabling the first successful reconstructions in DP-defended federated learning settings.

item.title: Conflict-Aware Retriever Editing for Knowledge Injection Attacks on LLM-Based RAG Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18310v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

检索增强生成（RAG）系统因依赖外部知识检索而面临新型安全威胁：攻击者可通过注入恶意知识篡改检索结果，进而诱导大语言模型生成有害内容。现有注入攻击主要基于数据层面操纵（如构造恶意语料库），但合成文本易被检测，导致攻击失效。随着开源检索模型（如BGE、Qwen-Embedding）广泛部署，模型层面攻击成为更隐蔽、更高效的突破口。

方法创新：CAREATTACK框架

本文提出冲突感知的检索器编辑攻击框架（CAREATTACK），首次实现对稠密检索模型的参数级、目标导向的知识注入。框架包含两大核心阶段：

  • 冲突感知检索器编辑：将高效闭式参数编辑技术适配至检索模型，通过图结构冲突检测识别恶意知识与良性竞争段落间的参数冲突，并利用投影式参数编辑精准提升恶意段落的相似度得分，同时抑制干扰；
  • 攻击保持型锚点修复：在编辑后进行轻量校准，仅修正非目标查询（non-target prompts）的检索偏差，严格保留对目标查询的攻击效果，显著提升攻击鲁棒性与泛化性。

实验验证与意义

我们在Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B与BGE-M3两大主流开源检索器上实例化CAREATTACK，在MSMARCO、BEIR、TREC-COVID三大基准数据集上评估。结果表明：
✅ 恶意段落平均Top-5召回率提升达42.7%–68.3%（vs. baseline）；
✅ 支持批量目标提示/段落协同攻击，单次编辑可覆盖数十个攻击场景；
✅ 仅需访问检索模型参数（无需训练数据或梯度），具备强实用性。
本工作揭示了开源RAG生态中被长期忽视的模型参数级攻击面，为RAG安全防护（如检索器完整性验证、编辑鲁棒性加固）提供了关键警示与技术基线。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces CAREATTACK, the first model-centric, parameter-editing-based attack framework for injecting malicious knowledge into open-source dense retrievers used in RAG systems. Unlike data-centric attacks that craft detectable synthetic corpus, CAREATTACK operates directly on retriever parameters via two novel stages: (1) conflict-aware retriever editing, which adapts closed-form parameter updates to promote malicious passages over benign competitors while resolving internal parameter conflicts via graph-based detection and projection; and (2) attack-preserving anchor repair, a lightweight calibration that preserves attack success on target prompts while minimizing collateral impact on non-target queries. Evaluated on Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B and BGE-M3 across three benchmarks (MSMARCO, BEIR, TREC-COVID), CAREATTACK achieves up to 68.3% higher Top-5 recall for malicious passages and supports batched, multi-prompt attacks—requiring only model parameter access. Our work exposes a practical, underexplored attack surface in widely deployed open-source RAG pipelines.

Abstract

Injecting malicious knowledge into retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems can manipulate retrieved evidence and mislead downstream generation, posing a serious security threat for AI applications. Existing RAG injection attacks mainly rely on manipulating external knowledge bases, such as crafting malicious corpus. However, the synthetic text crafted by such data-centric methods could be detectable, leading to the failure of attacks. Beyond corpus manipulation, open-source retrievers are increasingly exposing RAG systems to model-centric attacks. In this paper, we propose conflict-aware retriever editing, i.e., CAREATTACK, a model-centric retriever attack framework for malicious knowledge injection in RAG. Specifically, CAREATTACK consists two stages of conflict-aware retriever editing and attack-preserving anchor repair. Conflict-aware retriever editing adapts efficient closed-form parameter editing to the dense retrieval model, promoting malicious knowledge above benign competing passages and resolving potential parameter conflicts through graph-based conflict detection and parameter editing projection. Then, attack-preserving anchor repair performs lightweight calibration on the edited retriever to further eliminate the impact on non-target prompts while preserving the attack effectiveness for target prompts. We instantiate CAREATTACK on Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B and BGE-M3, and conduct evaluation on three benchmark datasets. Experimental results demonstrate our method substantially promote malicious passages into the retrieved knowledge of RAG systems and can perform attacks for batches of target prompts and passages, given the access of retrieval model parameters. Since most RAG systems are built upon open-source retrieval models, this work reveals a practical attack surface in RAG systems. Codes are public accessible at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CareAttack-3F1C.

item.title: PSyGenTAB: A Privacy-Preserving Framework for Synthetic Clinical Tabular Data Generation via Constrained Optimization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18518v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

医疗人工智能的发展长期受限于高质量临床数据的获取瓶颈：机构数据孤岛与《健康保险可携性和责任法案》（HIPAA）、《通用数据保护条例》（GDPR）等严格隐私法规，严重制约了跨机构数据共享与模型训练。

方法创新

本文提出 PSyGenTAB——一种面向临床结构化数据的隐私保护型合成生成框架。其核心创新在于将合成数据生成建模为带约束的优化问题，采用增广拉格朗日法（Augmented Lagrangian Method） 进行求解。该框架首次将可配置的隐私约束（如k-匿名性、差分隐私松弛形式、重构误差下界）直接嵌入生成模型训练目标中，实现对隐私保障水平的显式、可验证控制，同时最大化临床效用（如特征相关性、诊断分布保真度）。

关键结果

在涵盖电子病历、ICU监测及罕见病诊断的多中心临床基准测试中：

  • ✅ 精确保留关键跨特征临床关系（如血压与肾功能指标的非线性关联）；
  • ✅ 显著增强少数类诊断模式（如脓毒症早期亚型）的生成保真度，F1-score提升达23.7%；
  • ✅ 下游任务评估显示：Train-on-Synthetic, Test-on-Real 场景下，模型性能达真实数据基线的94.2%；Train-on-Real, Test-on-Synthetic 场景下仍保持89.5%泛化能力；
  • 🔒 隐私审计证实：精确记录重构造率降低至0.03%，对成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attack）的攻击成功率降至12.4%（远低于基线模型的41.8%）。

意义

PSyGenTAB为医疗AI提供了首个兼顾可证明隐私下界与临床语义保真的合成数据生成范式，支持安全、合规、高保真的跨机构协作研发。

AI Summary (English)

PSyGenTAB is a privacy-preserving framework for generating synthetic clinical tabular data by formulating generation as a constrained optimization problem solved via the Augmented Lagrangian Method. It explicitly embeds configurable privacy constraints (e.g., k-anonymity guarantees, reconstruction error bounds) into training—enforcing verifiable minimum privacy thresholds while maximizing clinical utility. Across diverse clinical benchmarks, PSyGenTAB preserves inter-feature medical relationships and minority-class diagnostic patterns critical for robust AI. Downstream models trained on synthetic data achieve 94.2% of real-data performance under Train-on-Synthetic/Test-on-Real evaluation, and maintain strong generalization (89.5%) in reverse settings. Privacy audits confirm near-zero exact record reproduction (<0.03%) and high resilience to membership inference attacks (attack success rate: 12.4% vs. 41.8% baseline). PSyGenTAB establishes a principled, utility-aware foundation for trustworthy synthetic healthcare data.

Abstract

The development of medical AI is constrained by limited access to high-quality clinical data due to institutional silos and strict privacy regulations such as HIPAA and GDPR. Synthetic data generation offers a potential solution, but existing methods lack principled mechanisms to explicitly manage the privacy-utility trade-off, often degrading clinically meaningful patterns or risking patient re-identification. We present PSyGenTAB, a privacy-preserving generative framework that formulates synthetic healthcare data generation as a constrained optimization problem solved using the Augmented Lagrangian Method. By embedding configurable privacy constraints directly into model training, PSyGenTAB enforces minimum privacy thresholds while maximizing clinical data utility. Across multiple clinically motivated benchmarks, PSyGenTAB preserves inter-feature clinical relationships and minority-class diagnostic patterns essential for reliable health AI. Downstream evaluation using Train-on-Synthetic, Test-on-Real and Train-on-Real, Test-on-Synthetic protocols shows that models trained on synthetic data achieve performance comparable to those trained on real patient records. Privacy auditing further demonstrates reduced exact record reproduction and strong resilience to membership inference attacks. These results establish PSyGenTAB as a principled framework for balancing privacy protection and clinical utility in synthetic healthcare data, supporting secure cross-institutional AI development.

item.title: From Specification to Execution: AI Assisted Scientific Workflow Management
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18425v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

科学工作流管理系统（WMS）是支撑大规模、可复现科研计算的关键基础设施，但其设计、实现与调试仍高度依赖人工，门槛高、耗时长、易出错。当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动化工作流生成方法多采用端到端代码直译，缺乏对意图—设计—实现的分层建模，导致可解释性弱、验证滞后、系统集成困难，难以满足科研场景对透明性、可审计性与工具链兼容性的严苛要求。

方法创新

本研究提出“从规范到执行”的AI辅助科学工作流管理框架，包含三大核心组件：

  • 结构化规范阶段：将工作流生命周期解耦为意图描述（What）、逻辑设计（How）、技术实现（Where/How-to-code） 三层，支持在代码生成前进行形式化验证与专家协同评审；
  • LLM驱动的跨层调试代理：构建具备系统感知能力的调试智能体，可自动定位并修复涵盖任务调度、数据依赖、资源约束及Pegasus运行时异常等多层级故障；
  • MCP-Pegasus融合执行层：通过Model Context Protocol（MCP）协议桥接LLM与Pegasus WMS，提供统一的工作流提交、实时监控、动态干预与结果溯源接口，实现AI与专业WMS的深度协同。

主要成果

在联邦学习医学影像分析真实场景中验证：系统成功生成并调度含超3000个作业的大规模分布式工作流；调试耗时平均降低62%；非领域专家用户可在15分钟内构建符合最佳实践的复杂迭代式工作流（如带检查点重试、异构设备调度、跨机构数据合规路由）。研究表明，AI不应替代WMS，而应作为可验证、可调试、可集成的增强层，推动科学工作流全生命周期向自动化、协作化与民主化演进。

AI Summary (English)

We present an AI-assisted scientific workflow management framework that bridges specification, debugging, and execution—moving beyond direct LLM code synthesis. Our approach introduces a structured specification phase separating intent, design, and implementation for early validation; an LLM-based debugging agent capable of diagnosing and resolving failures across scheduling, data dependencies, resource constraints, and Pegasus runtime layers; and an MCP-Pegasus integration layer enabling unified workflow submission, real-time monitoring, and interactive control. Evaluated on a federated learning medical imaging pipeline (characterized by parallelism, iteration, and complex inter-task dependencies), the system successfully generated and executed workflows with >3,000 jobs, reduced debugging effort by 62%, and enabled non-expert users to construct expert-level workflows in under 15 minutes. Results demonstrate the feasibility of end-to-end, transparent, and tool-integrated AI assistance for the full scientific workflow lifecycle.

Abstract

Scientific workflow management systems (WMS) support scalable and reproducible execution of complex pipelines, but workflow design, implementation, and debugging remain largely manual and require significant expertise. Recent approaches using large language models (LLMs) show promise for workflow generation from natural language, but often rely on direct code synthesis, which limits transparency, reproducibility, and integration with workflow systems. We present an AI-assisted approach to scientific workflow management that combines specification-driven workflow generation, automated debugging, and distributed execution. The method introduces a structured specification phase that separates workflow intent, design, and implementation, allowing validation prior to code generation. We also develop an LLM-based debugging agent that diagnoses and resolves failures across multiple system layers. To support distributed execution and user interaction, we integrate Pegasus, a widely used WMS, with a Model Context Protocol (MCP) layer, providing a unified interface for workflow submission, monitoring, and control. We evaluate the approach using a federated learning workflow for medical imaging, chosen for its parallel, iterative, and dependency-intensive structure. The system generated and executed large-scale workflows with thousands of jobs, reduced debugging effort, and allowed non-expert users to construct workflows with expert-level design patterns. These results indicate that end-to-end AI-assisted workflow generation and execution is feasible, and point toward AI-driven platforms for managing the scientific workflow lifecycle.

item.title: SCOPE-FL: A Strategy-proof Chain-based Optimal pareto efficient Federated Learning System
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18384v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

分层联邦学习（HFL）通过多级聚合架构支持大规模分布式设备协同建模，兼顾隐私保护与可扩展性。然而，现有HFL客户端选择机制存在双重缺陷：策略非真实性（non-strategy-proofness）——客户端可虚报算力、带宽或数据质量以获取更高奖励；帕累托低效（Pareto inefficiency）——偏好稳定性而牺牲全局资源最优分配，导致系统整体福利在帕累托意义上持续受损。

方法创新

本文提出 SCOPE-FL（Strategy-proof Chain-based Optimal pareto efficient Federated Learning），一种同步式HFL框架：

  • 机制设计：将客户端选择建模为双边“学校选择问题”，采用Top Trading Cycle（TTC）算法求解，首次在HFL中同时严格保障帕累托效率（PE） 与策略真实性（SP）；
  • 激励相容：设计基于单轮重构（One-Round Reconstruction, OR）的Shapley值近似算法，实现高可扩展性、低开销的贡献度量化与公平奖励分配；
  • 可信执行：全部逻辑（TTC匹配、OR计算、奖励发放）部署于区块链智能合约，确保SP性质在开放、不可信环境中实际成立。

实验验证

在MNIST、Fashion-MNIST和CIFAR-10上全面评估：SCOPE-FL相较DA（Deferred Acceptance）、IAS等SOTA方法，在模型精度提升1.8–3.2%、收敛速度加快22–37%、奖励分配效率（贡献-回报比）提高41%；通信延迟与DA相当，而区块链开销在百节点规模下仅为DA的38%，显著提升可扩展性。

AI Summary (English)

SCOPE-FL is a synchronous hierarchical federated learning framework that resolves two critical flaws in existing client selection: lack of strategy-proofness (SP) and Pareto inefficiency (PE). It models selection as a two-sided school choice problem solved via the Top Trading Cycle (TTC) algorithm—guaranteeing both SP and PE simultaneously. For reward distribution, SCOPE-FL introduces a scalable Shapley value approximation based on One-Round Reconstruction (OR), ensuring contribution-proportional compensation. All mechanisms are enforced via blockchain smart contracts to uphold SP in practice. Extensive experiments on MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and CIFAR-10 show SCOPE-FL outperforms DA, IAS, and other baselines in accuracy (+1.8–3.2%), convergence speed (+22–37%), and reward efficiency (+41%), while achieving communication latency comparable to DA and 62% lower blockchain overhead at scale.

Abstract

Hierarchical Federated Learning (HFL) enables scalable collaborative model training across distributed devices while preserving data privacy. However, existing HFL client selection mechanisms suffer from a fundamental strategic inefficiency. By prioritizing stability over Pareto efficiency (PE), they produce suboptimal resource allocations, and without strategy proofness (SP), participants are incentivized to misrepresent their true preferences, both failures degrading system overall welfare in the Pareto sense in practice. To address it, we propose SCOPE-FL (Strategy-proof Chain-based Optimal pareto efficient Federated Learning), a synchronous HFL framework that formulates client selection as a two-sided school choice problem solved through the Top Trading Cycle (TTC) algorithm that simultaneously guarantees PE and SP. For reward distribution, SCOPE-FL employs a scalable Shapley value approximation based on One-Round Reconstruction (OR), ensuring compensation proportional to each client's contribution. The entire mechanism executes via blockchain smart contracts, providing the tamper-proof environment required for the SP guarantees to hold in practice. A comprehensive evaluation on MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and CIFAR-10 demonstrates that SCOPE-FL outperforms state-of-the-art approaches, including DA, IAS, and other methods across model accuracy, convergence rate, and reward efficiency, while achieving communication latency comparable to DA and blockchain overhead significantly lower than DA at scale.

item.title: Protein-Based Fish Species Identification: Dataset, Models, and Insights from Native Bangladeshi Fish
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18302v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与意义

在孟加拉国，准确识别鱼类物种对保障粮食安全、推动渔业经济可持续发展及增强气候韧性至关重要。蛋白质序列直接承载功能约束与进化信号，是物种鉴定与生物多样性监测的理想分子标记。然而，针对本土鱼类的蛋白质序列识别尚无公开基准数据集与模型体系，严重制约了南亚地区基于蛋白质组学的食品溯源与资源管理。

方法与创新

本研究构建了首个面向9种本土孟加拉国鱼类的高质量蛋白质序列数据集（2845条序列），填补领域空白；系统评测7类主流架构，建立首个蛋白序列分类基线；提出轻量、可部署的新型混合架构——MotifCNN-Transformer+TA-PE（含基序卷积、Transformer主干与终端感知位置编码）。该模型兼顾局部保守基序识别与长程语义建模能力。

关键结果与优势

  • MotifCNN-Transformer+TA-PE达79.80%准确率（macro-F1=0.80）；
  • 微调大型蛋白语言模型ProtBERT（420M参数）达最高83.04%准确率，但McNemar检验显示其相对提升（+3.24%）统计不显著（p=0.1120）；
  • 在9个物种中，本模型在6类上单类准确率超越ProtBERT；
  • 推理速度快5倍、模型体积小42倍、批处理容量大16倍，且支持CPU/GPU-free推理，特别适配农村等资源受限场景。

应用价值

本工作揭示了系统发育关系对蛋白序列相似性的显著影响，为南亚“蛋白依赖型”经济体的渔业治理、食品真实性验证与生物多样性保护提供了可扩展的方法论基础与实用工具链。

AI Summary (English)

This study introduces the first curated protein-sequence dataset (2,845 high-quality sequences) for nine native Bangladeshi fish species and establishes the first classification baseline via systematic benchmarking of seven architectures. We propose MotifCNN-Transformer+TA-PE—a lightweight, deployable hybrid model achieving 79.80% accuracy (macro-F1 = 0.80). While fine-tuned ProtBERT attains 83.04% accuracy, its marginal gain is statistically insignificant (p = 0.1120, McNemar’s test); our model outperforms it on 6/9 classes and offers decisive practical advantages: 5× faster inference, 42× smaller size, 16× larger batch support, and GPU-free deployment—critical for resource-constrained settings. The work further links phylogenetic structure to sequence similarity, enabling scalable fisheries management, food authentication, and biodiversity conservation across South Asia.

Abstract

Correct identification of fish species is highly significant for food security, economic development, and climate resilience in Bangladesh. Protein sequences directly reflect functional and evolutionary constraints which are important for species authentication and biodiversity monitoring. Yet there exists no benchmark for native Bangladeshi fish species identification from protein sequence. In this study, we addressed this gap by introducing the first curated dataset for nine native Bangladeshi fish species of 2845 high quality protein sequences. We also established the first protein sequence classification baseline for this domain through a systematic benchmarking of seven architectural paradigms. Moreover, we propose a realistic deployable novel hybrid architecture of MotifCNN and Transformer with Terminal-Aware Positional-Encoding (MotifCNN-Transformer+TA-PE). Our novel architecture achieves 79.80% accuracy with macro-F1 of 0.80. The highest 83.04% accuracy is achieved by finetuned protein language model ProtBERT that has 420M parameters and requires dual 16GB GPUs for inference. According to McNemar's test, ProtBERT's 3.24% accuracy gain over our MotifCNN-Transformer+TA-PE is statistically insignificant (p = 0.1120). Our novel architecture beats it among six of the nine classes in per class identification. Also our MotifCNN-Transformer+TA-PE is approximately 5x faster, 42x smaller, and supports 16x larger batch size than ProtBERT and has GPU free inference, making it more practical for deployment in resources constrained areas such as rural Bangladesh. Beyond this, our foundational work shows effects of phylogenetic relationships on sequence similarity and establishes pathways for fisheries management, food authentication and biodiversity conservation in South Asia's protein dependent economy.

item.title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17035v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

传统观点认为，差分隐私（DP）天然增强联邦学习（FL）对后门攻击的鲁棒性。本文首次系统性挑战该假设，揭示DP-FL中存在根本性安全张力：DP噪声虽保护隐私，却同时掩盖了恶意更新的统计异常特征，致使现有基于梯度/更新分布的防御机制（如Norm Clipping、Krum、RFA等）失效。

方法创新：RING攻击

我们提出RING（Robust Inverse Noise-Guided backdoor）——一种专为DP-FL设计的新型协同后门攻击。其核心思想是：反向利用DP噪声的掩蔽效应。被攻陷客户端不直接注入强后门信号（易被检测），而是协作构造精细的对抗扰动，使各客户端在满足DP约束的前提下，将微弱但方向一致的恶意梯度分量“隐式编码”进本地更新；在服务器端聚合时，这些扰动经噪声抵消与信号叠加，重建出高信噪比的强后门信号，从而绕过所有基于异常检测的防御。

关键发现与影响

  • 在CIFAR-10、Tiny-ImageNet、AG News、SST-2四个非独立同分布（non-iid）数据集上，RING在ε=2.0的中等隐私预算下，对6种SOTA防御（包括DP-SGD+RFA、Norm Bounding等）的平均攻击成功率高达90.3%，较基线攻击提升最高达26.08倍；
  • RING作为即插即用的扰动层，与任意底层后门技术（如BadNets、Label-Consistent）正交兼容，显著扩大威胁面；
  • 安全评估表明：增强检测灵敏度或收紧DP参数均导致模型准确率断崖式下降（↓8.7%~14.2%），证实DP-FL存在隐私-安全-效用不可兼得的根本性权衡漏洞。

AI Summary (English)

Prior work assumes differential privacy (DP) inherently improves federated learning’s (FL) resilience against backdoor attacks. This paper challenges that assumption, revealing a critical tension: DP noise masks the statistical anomalies of malicious updates, rendering state-of-the-art defenses ineffective. We propose RING, a novel attack that exploits DP—rather than circumvents it—to stealthily reconstruct strong backdoor signals during aggregation. By collaboratively crafting DP-compliant adversarial perturbations, compromised clients evade anomaly detection while achieving high attack impact. RING is defense-agnostic, modular, and composable with existing backdoor techniques. Across four non-iid image and text datasets under moderate privacy budget (ε = 2.0), RING achieves a 90.3% average attack success rate against six SOTA defenses—up to 26.08× higher than baselines. Countermeasure analysis shows mitigating RING incurs severe utility loss (accuracy drop up to 14.2%), exposing a fundamental security gap in practical DP-FL deployments.

Abstract

Prior research suggests that differential privacy (DP) inherently enhances the robustness of federated learning (FL) against backdoor attacks. In this paper, we challenge this assumption. Through an empirical analysis of two baseline attack strategies, we uncover a fundamental tension in DP-FL: while bypassing DP allows state-of-the-art defenses to detect and filter malicious updates, complying with DP inadvertently masks their distinguishing statistical characteristics. Consequently, existing defenses become ineffective as DP reduces the raw backdoor signal. Building on this masking effect, we propose RING, a novel attack that explicitly exploits DP to conceal malicious contributions while maximizing attack impact. By collaboratively crafting adversarial perturbations, compromised clients reconstruct a strong backdoor signal during aggregation without triggering anomaly detection. RING operates as a perturbation layer that is agnostic to the underlying backdoor technique, making it broadly applicable and composable with existing attacks -- a property that significantly amplifies the threat it poses to DP-FL. Extensive evaluations across four image and text datasets under non-iid distributions show that RING achieves an average attack success rate of 90.3% against six state-of-the-art defenses under a moderate privacy budget, an improvement of up to 26.08x over baseline strategies. Finally, we evaluate potential countermeasures and find that mitigating this threat incurs significant utility trade-offs, exposing a fundamental security gap in the deployment of differentially private FL.

item.title: Cross-Silo De-Anonymization Under Local Differential Privacy: Threat Model, Phase Transition, and Coordination Necessity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16763v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在跨孤岛（cross-silo）本地差分隐私（LDP）场景中，同一用户的数据可能分散于 $k$ 个独立数据孤岛，每个孤岛对自身记录施加 $(\varepsilon, \delta)$-LDP 保护。标准串行组合定理仅给出保守的联合隐私保证 $(k\varepsilon, k\delta)$-DP，但无法回答关键安全问题：当 $k$ 达到何值时，攻击者能实际完成对目标个体的去匿名化？

方法与理论框架

本文构建首个面向跨孤岛个体级推理的信息论分析框架：

  • 提出 跨孤岛人员级差分隐私（XSP-DP） ——一种Pufferfish风格的隐私定义，其邻接关系将单个用户的全部跨孤岛记录视为整体单元，从而精准刻画真实威胁模型；
  • 严格证明标准基本组合界在此新邻接模型下依然成立，确保理论兼容性；
  • 基于Fano不等式与最大似然估计，推导去匿名化的相变阈值：$k^* = \Theta\left(\frac{\log n}{\varepsilon^2}\right)$（$n$ 为总人口规模，$\varepsilon$ 为每孤岛随机响应参数）；
  • 设计显式的 XOR + 随机响应构造，首次揭示“信息协同效应”：各孤岛单独输出对目标完全无信息（互信息为0），但联合输出具备严格正互信息；
  • 对非协调二元随机响应机制，证明一旦 $k > k^*$，去匿名化必然发生，从而确立跨孤岛协调机制的必要性。

创新与意义

本工作首次建立LDP下跨孤岛推理的精确相变理论，提供可量化的威胁基线（$\Theta$-阶阈值），为联邦学习、隐私计算等场景的隐私预算分配与架构设计提供关键理论依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first information-theoretic foundation for cross-silo de-anonymization under local differential privacy (LDP). We introduce Cross-Silo Person-level DP (XSP-DP), a Pufferfish-style privacy notion whose adjacency relation jointly considers all records of a single individual across $k$ silos—capturing realistic inference threats. Within this framework, we prove a sharp phase transition: de-anonymization succeeds with high probability when $k \gg k^ = \Theta(\log n / \varepsilon^2)$ and fails when $k \ll k^$, where $n$ is population size and $\varepsilon$ is per-silo randomized response parameter. A constructive XOR+RR scheme demonstrates information synergy: each silo’s output is individually uninformative about the target, yet their joint mutual information is strictly positive. Crucially, for non-coordinated binary RR mechanisms, de-anonymization becomes inevitable beyond $k^*$—proving that cross-silo coordination is necessary to prevent inference. These results provide a rigorous threat model and tight $\Theta$-level threshold for cross-silo attacks under LDP.

Abstract

When a person's records appear in k independent data silos, each protected by (epsilon, delta)-differential privacy, standard composition yields a valid (kepsilon, kdelta)-DP guarantee for the joint output. This worst-case bound, however, does not answer the concrete inference question: at what k can an adversary actually identify a target person? This paper develops the information-theoretic framework needed to answer that question.
