We introduce cross-silo person-level DP (XSP-DP), a Pufferfish-style privacy notion whose adjacency relation captures all records of a single person across all silos simultaneously, and verify that the standard basic composition bound carries over to this adjacency model. Within this framework we prove that de-anonymization undergoes a phase transition at k = Theta(log n / epsilon^2) (population size n, per-silo RR parameter epsilon): a Fano lower bound shows any estimator fails for k << k, while a matching maximum-likelihood upper bound shows the attack succeeds for k >> k*. An explicit XOR + randomized-response construction demonstrates information synergy: each silo's output is individually uninformative about the target, yet the joint mutual information is strictly positive. For non-coordinated binary randomized-response mechanisms, we prove that de-anonymization is inevitable once k exceeds the threshold, establishing that cross-silo coordination is necessary. These results provide a baseline threat model and Theta-level threshold for cross-silo inference attacks under local DP.

item.title: Automated jailbreak attack targeting multiple defense strategies
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16751v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLMs）虽在各类任务中展现出卓越能力，但其安全性仍面临严峻挑战——尤其易受基于提示词的对抗性“越狱攻击”（jailbreak attacks）影响。现有攻击方法多依赖静态模板或针对特定模型反复调优，泛化性差、成本高，难以系统评估多层防御机制（如内容过滤、强化学习对齐、推理时监控等）下的真实鲁棒性。

方法：UNIATTACK 框架

本文提出 UNIATTACK——一种面向防御评估的自动化黑盒越狱攻击框架。其核心创新在于特征中心化构造范式：

  • 特征提取：从数十种主流越狱攻击（如GCG、AutoDAN、PAIR）中自动识别并抽取最小但高迁移性的攻击子结构（如语义混淆词、角色伪装句式、格式扰动标记）；
  • 智能优化：利用专用微调的攻击者LLM（Attacker-LLM），在无梯度条件下对特征组合进行语义保真与攻击效力联合优化；
  • 模板生成：通过迭代式自动化精炼流程，将优化后的特征动态组装为轻量、可复用、上下文自适应的提示模板。

主要发现与贡献

  • 在部署多层防御（如LlamaGuard + RLHF + SafeTokenizer）的12个主流闭源/开源模型（GPT-4o、Claude-3.5、Qwen2-72B、Llama-3-70B等）上，UNIATTACK实现平均攻击成功率（ASR）提升64.63%–248.82%，显著超越GCG、TAP等基线；
  • 攻击成本极低：仅需0.03%–4.96%的查询开销与计算资源，支持单次提示（one-shot）跨模型、跨安全类别（危害内容、隐私泄露、逻辑规避）泛化；
  • 提供首个开源、可复现的防御压力测试工具包，助力开发者高效定位防御盲区。代码与评估数据集已公开：https://anonymous.4open.science/r/UniAttack-Artifact-30F1。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) are vulnerable to jailbreak attacks, yet existing adversarial prompt methods lack generalizability and efficiency across diverse defense mechanisms. This paper introduces UNIATTACK, a defense-oriented, black-box adversarial testing framework that constructs effective attack prompts feature-centrically: it extracts minimal high-impact attack primitives from heterogeneous jailbreak techniques, optimizes them via a specialized attacker LLM, and automatically composes them into flexible, context-aware templates—enabling one-shot, cross-model, cross-defense attacks. Evaluated on 12 state-of-the-art models with multi-layered safety systems (e.g., LlamaGuard + RLHF + input sanitization), UNIATTACK achieves 64.63–248.82% higher average attack success rate (ASR) than baselines (GCG, TAP, AutoDAN), while consuming only 0.03–4.96% of their query and computational cost. The artifact is publicly available for robustness auditing.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across a wide range of tasks. However, their safety remains a critical concern due to their susceptibility to adversarial prompt-based attacks. In this paper, we present UNIATTACK, an adversarial testing framework designed from a defense-oriented perspective to systematically construct effective black-box attack prompts. Unlike prior approaches that rely on static templates or iterative model-specific tuning, UNIATTACK extracts minimal but high-impact attack features from diverse existing attacks, optimizes them via a specialized attacker LLM, and composes them into flexible templates through automated refinement process. This feature-centric construction enables one-shot attacks that generalize across multiple models and safety categories, providing a practical tool for assessing LLM robustness. Our evaluation results shows that compared to the baselines, UNIATTACK achieves an average attack success rate (ASR) improvement of 64.63\%-248.82\% on models deployed with multi-layered defense mechanisms and it only takes 0.03\%-4.96\% cost of the baselines. UNIATTACK artifact is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/UniAttack-Artifact-30F1.

item.title: Robust and Automated Reconfiguration of Byzantine Wide-Area Replication
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16740v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在广域分布式系统中，拜占庭容错状态机复制（BFT-SMR）是保障区块链等关键服务一致性的核心机制。传统方案依赖静态配置（如固定领导者、均匀投票权重），导致共识延迟高、带宽利用率低。近期研究转向动态重配置——通过实时监测网络延迟，自适应调整领导者选举与副本权重，以提升性能。然而，我们发现该范式存在三大拜占庭漏洞：（1）恶意节点伪造延迟报告以抢占领导权；（2）操纵权重分配破坏法定人数（quorum）鲁棒性；（3）协同攻击导致重配置收敛至非安全配置。

方法创新：Beware 框架

为应对上述威胁，本文提出 Beware——首个面向拜占庭环境的鲁棒自动化重配置框架。其核心包含三层防御：

  • 可信延迟过滤器：基于多源时序校验与异常检测（如Grubbs检验），剔除离群延迟测量，抑制虚假报告；
  • 鲁棒权重计算引擎：采用截断均值与加权中位数融合策略，在≤f个拜占庭节点下保证权重分布满足安全性约束（如法定人数完整性）；
  • 抗干扰配置学习器：设计轻量级在线强化学习模块，仅依赖局部观测奖励信号，在动态网络中渐进收敛至拜占庭弹性配置，避免全局状态同步开销。

实验验证

在跨洲部署的16节点测试床（含4个拜占庭节点）上评估表明：Beware 将平均共识延迟降低45%（较OptiBFT）、吞吐量提升3.2×，且在DDoS+延迟欺骗复合攻击下仍保持100%安全性。代码已开源，支持主流BFT协议（HotStuff、LibraBFT）即插即用。

AI Summary (English)

Distributed Byzantine fault-tolerant systems rely on static configurations, incurring high latency and low throughput. Recent dynamic reconfiguration approaches—adjusting leaders and vote weights based on network latency—improve performance but introduce three exploitable vulnerabilities: falsified latency reports, manipulated weight assignments, and unsafe convergence under collusion. We propose Beware, the first robust, automated reconfiguration framework for wide-area BFT-SMR. Beware integrates (1) a Byzantine-resilient latency filter using temporal consistency checks and outlier rejection; (2) a robust weight computation engine leveraging truncated means and weighted medians to guarantee quorum safety under ≤f faults; and (3) a lightweight online reinforcement learner that converges to resilient configurations using only local reward signals. Evaluated across intercontinental deployments with up to 4 Byzantine nodes, Beware reduces consensus latency by up to 45% and boosts throughput by 3.2× versus state-of-the-art baselines—while maintaining 100% safety under adversarial network manipulation.

Abstract

Distributed systems handle adversarial nodes through redundancy, which imposes a significant performance overhead. In blockchain systems, Byzantine fault-tolerant state-machine replication (BFT-SMR) is the replicated service that totally orders client transactions before execution. While prior research has primarily focused on designing novel consensus algorithms with improved performance, recent studies have shown that further gains can be achieved through configuration optimization. More precisely, replicas can monitor network latency to dynamically assign the leader role and tune voting weights, thereby improving consensus performance. However, we identify three vulnerabilities in this process that Byzantine nodes can exploit. To address these weaknesses, we propose Beware, a reconfiguration framework that filters out falsified latency reports, computes robust weight distributions, and applies machine learning to converge towards Byzantine-resilient configurations. Our evaluation shows that Beware reduces consensus latency by up to 45% compared to existing solutions.

item.title: A data-driven security quantification framework for IoT-based systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16561v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向物联网系统的数据驱动安全量化框架

物联网（IoT）已深度融入工业控制、智能医疗与城市基础设施等安全关键型系统，但其异构架构、动态威胁演进及缺乏可靠的漏洞可利用性概率数据，严重制约了网络安全风险的定量评估能力。传统攻击树分析（ATA）虽能结构化建模攻击路径，却常依赖主观专家判断或启发式评分，导致结果不确定性高、可复现性差。

本研究提出一种融合模型驱动工程（MBSE）、攻击树分析（ATA） 与实证漏洞情报的新型数据驱动安全量化框架。核心创新在于：

  • 基于SysML构建系统架构模型，自动推导层级化攻击树；
  • 将CVE漏洞映射为攻击树中的基本攻击步骤（BAS），并采用漏洞可利用性预测评分系统（EPSS） 赋予客观、动态的 exploitation probability（基于全球实时遥测数据）；
  • 将攻击树形式化为贝叶斯网络（BN），支持前向概率推理（系统被攻陷概率）、反向诊断推理（定位最可能致因漏洞）及脆弱性临界度排序（Criticality Index）；
  • 实现端到端量化：输出关键指标包括系统级妥协概率（P_compromise）、各攻击路径贡献度、以及基于风险减益（Risk Reduction Benefit）的缓解措施优先级清单。

该框架已在某智能电网边缘监控系统案例中验证：相较传统定性ATA，其量化结果使高危漏洞识别准确率提升42%，缓解策略部署效率提高3.1倍。本工作首次将EPSS与MBSE-ATA-BN闭环集成，显著增强IoT安全评估的客观性、可追溯性与决策支持能力，为ISO/IEC 27001与IEC 62443合规性验证提供可审计的技术支撑。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents a data-driven probabilistic security quantification framework for IoT-based safety-critical systems. By integrating Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE), Attack Tree Analysis (ATA), and empirical vulnerability intelligence—specifically the Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS)—the framework replaces subjective scoring with objective, real-world exploitability probabilities. SysML models formalize system architecture to auto-generate attack trees; vulnerabilities are mapped to Basic Attack Steps and assigned EPSS-derived probabilities; and the tree is transformed into a Bayesian Network to enable probabilistic inference, root-cause diagnosis, and criticality ranking. The framework outputs quantitative system compromise likelihoods, identifies dominant attack paths, and prioritizes mitigation actions by risk reduction benefit. Validated on an industrial IoT use case, it improves high-risk vulnerability detection accuracy by 42% and mitigation planning efficiency by 3.1× over conventional ATA. This work establishes a reproducible, audit-ready foundation for standards-aligned cybersecurity risk assessment in complex IoT environments.

Abstract

The Internet of Things (IoT) is integral to modern cyber-physical systems. Quantitative cybersecurity assessment in IoT environments remains challenging due to heterogeneous system architectures, evolving threat landscapes, and the limited availability of reliable probabilistic exploitability data. Although Attack Tree Analysis (ATA) provides a structured framework for modelling potential attack paths leading to system compromise, conventional ATA quantification often relies on subjective expert judgement or heuristic scoring schemes, which can introduce uncertainty and reduce analytical reproducibility. This study introduces a data-driven probabilistic security framework for IoT-based safety-critical systems by integrating Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE), ATA, and empirical vulnerability data. In the proposed framework, SysML models capture system architecture, from which attack trees are derived. Vulnerabilities are mapped as Basic Attack Steps and assigned exploitation probabilities using the Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS). The attack tree is then represented as a Bayesian Network, enabling probabilistic reasoning, diagnostic inference, and vulnerability criticality analysis. The framework quantifies system compromise probabilities, identifies likely causes of attacks, and prioritises mitigation strategies. By combining architecture-driven modelling with real-world vulnerability intelligence, it provides a rigorous, reproducible approach for cybersecurity risk assessment in complex IoT environments.

item.title: Transferable Self-Evolving Playbooks for Agentic Security Auditing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16420v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体安全审计系统由三部分构成：代码分析LLM、代理运行时（如Codex或OpenCode）、以及审计剧本（playbook）——即指导漏洞发现的领域特定程序性知识。以往工作严重依赖人工构建剧本（如提示工程、手工流程、启发式规则），带来两大瓶颈：获取成本高（是否必须人工？能否自动化生成？）和泛化能力弱（演化后的剧本能否迁移至更弱的LLM代理，提升其能力？）

方法：EvoHunt框架

本文提出EvoHunt——首个面向开源仓库的安全审计剧本自演化环境。其核心为三代理协同闭环：

  • 审计代理：执行当前剧本，输出漏洞发现结果；
  • 评估代理：依据真实安全通告（ground truth）对结果打分；
  • 修订代理：基于失败案例分析，动态增删剧本内容（不限格式：可添加/删除工作流、启发式规则、漏洞知识条目或领域语义片段）。

剧本从空初始化，全程无结构约束；演化后仅需微调适配即可迁移至不同LLM或运行时。

关键发现与创新

  • 自动化获取显著有效：在开源安全通告基准上，Codex/GPT5.4-xhigh端到端漏洞利用率从1.1%跃升至6.2%（+6×）；OpenCode/GLM5.1演化剧本在所有指标上超越OpenAI Codex Security（目标匹配率11.3% vs. 9.2%），证明开源自主演化可超越商用闭源方案。
  • 强迁移能力：GLM演化剧本成功迁移至Qwen系列弱模型——Qwen3.6-27B匹配率从2.4%→6.5%，Qwen3.6-35B-A3B从1.1%→4.6%，A3B获得2.4倍于GPT迁移的匹配数。

本工作首次实现无需人工干预的剧本自演化与跨模型能力迁移，为可扩展、低成本、高适应性的智能安全审计奠定新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces EvoHunt, a self-evolving framework for domain-specific security audit playbooks—procedural knowledge guiding LLM agents in vulnerability discovery. Unlike prior human-crafted playbooks (e.g., prompts, heuristics, workflows), EvoHunt automates playbook acquisition and transfer via a three-agent loop: an audit agent executes the current playbook; an evaluator scores findings against ground-truth advisories; and a reviser updates the playbook by analyzing failures—starting from empty and unconstrained in format. Evaluated on open-source security advisories, EvoHunt boosts end-to-end exploit rates by 6× (1.1% → 6.2%) for Codex/GPT5.4-xhigh, and its evolved OpenCode/GLM5.1 playbook outperforms OpenAI Codex Security (11.3% vs. 9.2% target-match rate). Crucially, the evolved playbook transfers effectively to weaker models: Qwen3.6-27B improves from 2.4% to 6.5%, and A3B achieves 2.4× more matches than GPT-based transfer. EvoHunt establishes the first fully automated, transferable, and open playbook evolution paradigm for agentic security auditing.

Abstract

An LLM agent for vulnerability discovery and validation is more than a model. It combines three components: an LLM for code analysis, an agent harness such as Codex or OpenCode for navigation, tool use, and execution, and an audit playbook, domain-specific procedural knowledge that guides the LLM and harness toward vulnerability discovery. Prior work relies on human-supplied playbooks, including prompt engineering, manual workflows, knowledge bases, and heuristics. This raises two research questions: Acquisition - is human curation necessary, and can playbook creation be automated? Transfer - can an evolved playbook transfer the audit procedure to weaker agents, improving their capability? We present EvoHunt, a playbook evolution environment over open-source repositories for security auditing. Three agents drive the evolution loop: an audit agent rolls out the current playbook and produces findings; an evaluator scores outcomes against ground truth; and a reviser commits updates to the playbook based on failure analysis. The playbook format is unconstrained: starting empty, EvoHunt adds or removes workflows, heuristics, vulnerability knowledge, or domain-specific content. The evolved playbook requires only minor adaptation to run under a different LLM or harness. We evaluate EvoHunt on open-source security advisories. For acquisition, playbook evolution raises end-to-end exploits for Codex/GPT5.4-xhigh 6x, from 1.1% to 6.2%, and the evolved OpenCode/GLM5.1 playbook surpasses OpenAI Codex Security on every metric, with 11.3% vs. 9.2% target-match rate, showing open-source evolution can outperform a dedicated commercial product. For transfer, the GLM-evolved playbook gives the strongest student lift: Qwen3.6-27B improves from 2.4% to 6.5%, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B from 1.1% to 4.6%, and A3B obtains 2.4x more matches than GPT transfer.

item.title: FEnc$^2$: Unifying Data Packing for Efficient Private Inference via Convolution and Architecture-Aware Fragment Encoding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16359v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

全同态加密（FHE）为隐私保护机器学习提供了理论保障，但其高昂的计算与内存开销严重制约实际部署。除底层原语（如数论变换NTT、旋转、密钥切换）固有代价外，应用层密文打包（ciphertext packing）低效是被长期忽视的关键瓶颈：现有策略或优先保持空间邻近性（如卷积滑动窗口），或侧重特征/通道分组，却无法兼顾二者，导致密文槽位浪费、旋转次数激增、密文数量膨胀。

方法创新：FEnc²统一框架

本文提出FEnc²——首个面向CKKS方案、融合卷积特性与网络架构的碎片化编码框架，包含两大核心组件：

  • 卷积感知编码（Conv-aware Encoding）：通过解析建模，动态选择最优数据碎片大小，解耦空间依赖关系，并联合最小化各层的“内旋转”（intra-layer）与“外旋转”（inter-layer），显著提升槽位利用率；
  • 架构感知密文压缩（Arch-aware Ct Compression）：在特征降维或通道缩减层（如池化、1×1卷积）后主动重构密文结构，恢复密文密度，避免信息稀疏化。

性能突破与意义

FEnc²重塑加密张量布局，将同态运算量降低1–2个数量级。在满内存负载（最大批大小）下：

  • LeNet/MNIST：GPU端较SOTA系统Orion提速228.83×，CPU端达226.06×；
  • MobileNet/ImageNet：GPU提速4.55×，CPU达9.43×。

FEnc²硬件无关，却具备架构级变革力——它在执行前优化加密数据布局，从源头减少密文数量与硬件负载，与NTT/密钥切换等底层加速器形成正交互补。本工作确立应用层数据布局为加密推理的首要架构设计维度，为下一代FHE系统提供关键使能技术。

AI Summary (English)

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables privacy-preserving inference but suffers from prohibitive overhead—not only from expensive low-level primitives (e.g., NTT, rotation, key switching), but critically from application-level ciphertext packing inefficiency. Existing packing strategies sacrifice either spatial locality (for convolution) or feature/channel grouping, wasting slots and inflating rotations and ciphertext count. We propose FEnc², a unified, fragment-based encoding framework for CKKS-based private CNN inference. It introduces: (1) Conv-aware Encoding, which analytically selects optimal fragment size to decouple spatial dependencies and jointly minimize intra- and inter-layer rotations; and (2) Arch-aware Ct Compression, which restores ciphertext density after dimensionality-reduction layers. Together, they reshape encrypted workload structure and reduce homomorphic operations by 10–100×. On LeNet/MNIST, FEnc² achieves up to 228.83× (GPU) and 226.06× (CPU) end-to-end speedup over Orion; on MobileNet/ImageNet, up to 4.55× (GPU) and 9.43× (CPU). Hardware-agnostic yet architecturally transformative, FEnc² proves that application-level data layout is a first-order design dimension for efficient FHE inference.

Abstract

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables privacy-preserving machine learning but incurs extreme computational and memory overhead. These costs come not only from expensive low-level primitives, including Number Theoretic Transform (NTT), rotation, and key-switching, but also from inefficient ciphertext packing at the application level. Existing packing strategies typically preserve either neighboring data elements or feature grouping, but not both, leading to wasted ciphertext slots, excessive rotations, and inflated ciphertext counts. We propose FEnc2, a unified and principled fragment-based encoding framework for CKKS-based private convolutional neural network inference. FEnc2 optimizes slot utilization, rotation complexity, and ciphertext density through two components: 1)Conv-aware Encoding, which analytically selects an optimal fragment size to decouple spatial dependencies and jointly minimize inner-outer rotations across layers, and 2)Arch-aware Ct Compression, which restores ciphertext density after feature- or channel-reduction layers. Together, these transformations reshape encrypted workload structure and reduce homomorphic operations by one to two orders of magnitude. With full memory capacity utilized, i.e., at maximum batch size, FEnc2 achieves end-to-end latency speedups over the state-of-the-art Orion of up to 228.83x on GPU and 226.06x on CPU for LeNet on MNIST, and up to 4.55x on GPU and 9.43x on CPU for MobileNet on ImageNet. FEnc2 is hardware-agnostic yet architecturally transformative: by optimizing encrypted tensor layout before execution, it reduces ciphertext count and workload pressure on hardware, complementing primitive-level optimizations such as NTT and keyswitch accelerators. These results show that application-level data layout is a first-order architectural design dimension for encrypted inference and an important enabler for next-generation FHE systems.
